Fantastic Metroidvania souls-like. This has become one of my favorite games of all time, I have beaten the game more times that I would like to admit (I had the Epic Games version, but got it here for the dlc, and save files do work from version to version) It has fantastic artwork and music, the story is simple but easy to follow. The gameplay is fun and the controls are super responsive (I played it on keyboard and mouse) I never felt like Sorum ignored my inputs. The game is not that long but not that short either which encourages replayability to try the other classes and play styles. The Bosses are all really good, specially the end game ones (except for Heroic Eldritch Council, that one sucked) and I love that you can re-fight them whenever you like without having to go into ng+.  That being said there are some things that could have been better: - Most npcs feel irrelevant, specially Nymeria and Grimguad, they are just trainers for the most part. I also don't like that you can just kill them with them posing no resistance when npcs like Vrael actually fight you back if you try to kill him. Although I understand that it's mostly because unique sprites and combat mechanic for them would have been tons of extra work. - You can never have a run where you killed all the bosses, depending on your choice you are going to miss on some unless you do the boss rush mode from the dlc, and even then it will not registered as you defeated them. - Crafting the immortalite stones and meteor scraps is just tedious, if you want to fully upgrade your gear to +10 get ready to farm the heroic version of the Bulwark, and then go to the shop to get armor scraps and then to Alistar to turn them into the upgrade material... I believe that instead of armor scraps the material required to craft immortalite stones should have been just 10 soul stones, because they literally become useless very fast, most of my playthroughs ended up with me holding 800+ soul stones, and while you can consume them for currency, you have to do it one at a time... ONE AT A TIME. - Despite the Afterlife update, some areas still feel unfinished or that something is missing. - Heroic Eldritch Council... the only boss I dread to fight every single time, I rather fight the true final boss on heroic 10 times before that one.  The DLC was a bit of a letdown, only get it if you really want to Fight ASH or a boss rush mode. It basically adds a new challenge zone, 1 new boss, an arena and some challenge modes. I feel that there was a huge missed opportunity here as the devs could have combine all of the content to make a new big level instead, Imagine if you start the dlc, you get the first Vados area with the mages, but when you enter the castle, surprise, it has been corrupted into that challenge leve "Halls of Cruelty" so you have to traverse it until half way through you are challenged by a "mini boss" being that Arena where you fight waves of enemies, then you continue into the second half of Halls of Cruelty and when you reach the top you face against the final boss of the dlc. But instead all that content has been split into other parts of the map. Halls of Cruelty: It's only challenging if you don't know about specific tricks you can use to basically negate all challenge from it, like equipping a dark weapon for the beetles, using the wings of exile on the final + gaian blood to infinitely fly up, and respec into the noble class to abuse the "Treasure Hunter" glitch that allows you to infinite heal. The only rewards for completing the area are 2 tomes for the final boss, an upgrade which I'm not sure it carries to NG+ and some seeds of knowledge. Arena of Blood: Good, but the boss rush skips some bosses, maybe for story reasons. And some of the waves of enemies are simply frustrating instead of fun (the slimes that poison you). The challenge modes: fun but not what I expected. The randomizer just gives you the same items over and over, aside from leaves of gaia and some unique named items there's not that many drops that are worth it, I was expecting boss weapons, auras, maybe unique weapon variants or at least the ocational fully upgraded weapon. The forced Heroics still require you to fight the regular versions of the bosses, I was under the impression you would have to fight the heroic ones from the get go. Also it seems that it doesn't work on some bosses at all, like the Wraith King. The fink mode is hilarious at first but that's about it. The challenge that breaks your gear upon dead is fun if you combine it with the randomizer. I wish there was other challenge modes though. Vados and the Final Boss of the DLC: The Vados area is just 1 screen with nothing going on aside from an annoying mage enemy. I feel that they could have reused assets to make a more interesting level. I was expecting something longer like the "???" level. The boss himself is good, his reward for defeating him is insanely good, funny enough the Heroic version of the fight is actually easier than the regular one, only because you have breaks between the phases, he's also super easy once you learn the timings for deflecting his attacks.  That's about it, despite its flaws it is still a fantastic game and you absolutely should give it a try.

Хотел поставить негативный отзыв, но под конец игры передумал, и с натяжкой поставлю своё "да".  1. В игре красивая графика, приятная озвучка героев (кроме главного героя, который начинает говорить только в конце. Я этот прикол не оценил).   2. Разнообразные локации, интересные механики мира и битвы с боссами.  3. В игре куча секретов, и даже супер секретный босс, нахождение которого из разряда чего-то непонятного.  Однако в игре куда больше минусов:  0. Странное решение прыжка на кнопку вверх  1. Карта представляет собой кишку, и вместо нормального телепорта мы пол игры будет вынуждены ходить по невзрачному коридору на лошадке.  2. В игре полно разных классов, но по большей части я не понимаю зачем оно нужно. Будучи магом ходил со стандартными заклинаниями всю игру, и ничего нового и интересного не выучил.  3. Просто куча рассходников, которые вы используете раза 2 за всю игру.  4. Душно-запутанный мир. Почти всё на старте будет утыкано дверьми, что открываются только с другой стороны. На каком-то этапе я тупо потерялся и не знал куда мне идти.  5. Нас всю игру пытаются убедить что мы дружим с ящеркой, но лично я к ней так и не проникся.  6. Странные скачки интонации. Вот вроде жестокость и страдания, а вот у нас викторина и хиханьки.   Так же нам предлагают сразить всех боссов второй раз в героическом режиме, но большинство из них это что-то сумасшедшее, по крайней мере на моем классе...  По итогу, игру скорее советую по большой скидке. Видно что люди старались, вкладывали душу в проект. Банально обидно за такое детище ставить негативную оценку, но средних в стиме нету.

Mixed. I just have mixed feelings. By all accounts I should love this game. I like souls-likes. The art is pretty. The setting is interesting. The voice acting is good. There are cute little tongue-in-cheek moments which made me laugh.  I'm truly not sure what it is, but something is just...off...with the game. The combat feels pretty bad, where your character is very quick, but attacks come out slowly. The feedback when attacking and being attacked by enemies is pretty clunky.  While the world is cool, I really feel like I know nothing about it. Maybe I need to read more item descriptions? But I'm just not sure what anything is, or what I'm trying to do.  Getting around is a bit of a pain. And the UI is really bad.  There's a talent tree but it's full of very minor effects. The weapons and armour don't really feel different.  I'm really not sure what the issue is. A lot of these things are true for Dark Souls. But they just...made it work? I'm not sure.

A fun an engaging game. Extremely modelled off Dark Souls, from NPC gloom and info, to the timing and boss pattern learning. However it has fresh elements, like the talents, unexpected sci-fi, and a unique colour style to it. This game has the right 'feel' and flow for a sword and dodging game. Music is great, suits the areas and the enemies perfectly. If you like Dark Souls and any other Metroidvania game, you'll love it. Play on controller is a must.

I am not a fan of disliking a game because I know that the reviews help the developer team, but I really like this genre and this game just couldn't do it for me. I spent the whole game using the same knifes I got from the second boss I beat. And not by choice, but because that was the only one. I only ever saw another knife when I was lvl 80 or something. The enemies have weird animation and some times you don't know if the attack will connect or not and you can't defend because the angle is weird. The combat should be a big part of the game, but that was one of the things it also didn't land. I also got stuck a couple of times because nothing was really intuitive, getting the fast travel only before beating the game was something that dragged it for the 12 hours it says I played. It felt like more. Half was probably dying over and over again and getting to a boss fight without life or plumes. Anyway. I liked the design, it was just not delivered in a good intuitive way. Another example: I had multiple skill points free and unlocked the second class. I chose Mage and never in the game found a lightning magic to use. I just had a book as a secondary weapon and that's it. I beat the game and still don't know how to actually be a mage.

Mediocre game, in my opinion.  There are much better metroidvanias and much better dark souls type games out there.  The combat and weapon variety initially are really fun, but the balancing is way off and very quickly I "outleveled" the game, to the point where I one-shot the last three bosses, which totally defeats the purpose of a "hardcore" game. (Didn't finish the game, so maybe the balancing becomes better towards the end, but I doubt it).  Initially liked the lore and setting but two things ruined the atmosphere and immersion. The beginning is all fantasy and medieval themed and suddenly you meet a sci-fi sniper boss, WTF? Secondly, at the times the game takes itself super serious and goes dark and grim and suddenly it becomes mega goofy and silly with some of the NPCs. Either way would have been fine but the combination is just non-sense.  Lastly, there are some mechanics that are just frustrating to be frustrating. For example, I finished a quest for a NPC in the hub area, which resulted in her a) giving me a useless cape and b) dying, so I lost access to her spells and abilities. What was the point?  Or the horrible "will" mechanic that many other reviewers pointed out: if you die too often you lock yourself out of the good endings, which takes all the fun out of experimenting with different weapons, abilities or set-ups because you get punished so hard for failing.

ENG/ITA REVIEW  I underestimated Death’s Gambit Afterlife for years, since its mixed reviews and somewhat low profile i always assumed it was a mediocre attempt at a 2D soulslike, one of my favorite genres. But i finally gave it a chance… and you should too.  At first glance, it plays like a standard soulslike set in a dark fantasy setting, then the game progresses, and it starts weaving in humorous undertones that, surprisingly, didn’t feel out of place, only for then change completely into something unexpected and the story begins to spiral into something much deeper and stranger than i ever anticipated.  What seemed like a straightforward fantasy narrative transforms into an experience full of complex, layered themes. Without spoiling anything, I’ll just say it’s far more futuristic and philosophical than it initially appears. Somehow, despite the wildly diverse topics it touches on, the game manages to keep everything coherent, even emotionally resonant. Toward the end, it all clicks in a way that’s both dramatic and genuinely moving.  The final acts of the game are incredibly well made. The music, boss fights, and weight of the path you have walked all come together in an epic crescendo. There’s even moments that breaks the fourth wall in a way i never saw coming but brilliantly implemented.  Gameplay wise, combat feels more fluid than Salt and Sanctuary, though not as fast paced as Grime, it's a solid middle ground. There’s a decent range of builds and abilities to explore, though some of the heroic boss fights felt unbalanced but that might just be my skill level.  This game surprised me on every level. Don’t sleep on it.  RECENSIONE IN ITALIANO:  Per anni ho sottovalutato Death’s Gambit Afterlife. A causa delle recensioni contrastanti e della sua bassa notorietà, ho sempre pensato fosse un tentativo mediocre di creare un soulslike 2D, uno dei miei generi preferiti. Ma alla fine ho deciso di dargli una possibilità… e dovresti farlo anche tu.  A prima vista, sembra un classico soulslike ambientato in un contesto dark fantasy. Poi, man mano che si avanza, il gioco introduce toni umoristici che, sorprendentemente, non stonano affatto. E proprio quando pensi di aver capito dove vuole andare a parare, tutto cambia: la storia si trasforma in qualcosa di molto più profondo e strano di quanto avessi mai immaginato.  Quella che sembrava una narrazione fantasy piuttosto lineare diventa un’esperienza ricca di temi complessi e stratificati. Senza fare spoiler, posso solo dire che è molto più futuristico e filosofico di quanto sembri inizialmente. E nonostante tocchi argomenti molto diversi tra loro, riesce comunque a mantenere una coerenza sorprendente, arrivando persino a toccare corde emotive. Verso la fine, tutto si incastra in modo drammatico e davvero toccante.  Gli atti finali del gioco sono realizzati in maniera eccellente. Le musiche, le boss fight e il percorso che hai sceltoculminano in un crescendo epico. Ci sono persino momenti in cui viene rotta la quarta parete in modo totalmente inaspettato, ma perfettamente sensato con alcuni dei temi sopracitati.  Per quanto riguarda il gameplay, il combattimento è più fluido di Salt and Sanctuary, anche se meno frenetico di Grime, un buon equilibrio. C’è una discreta varietà di build e abilità da provare, anche se alcune boss fight eroiche mi sono sembrate sbilanciate… ma forse è solo una questione di git gud.  Questo gioco mi ha sorpreso sotto ogni aspetto. Non ignoratelo.

It's not a full thumbs up, not a full thumbs down.  It's a 6.5/10 below average 2D Metroidvania Soulslike.  If you like this kind of game you'll enjoy your playthrough, but there's just so many problems....  The graphics aren't one of them.  Enemies and animations look pretty good.  The combat system is decent.  Hitting enemies gives soul energy which you can use for special attacks or maneuvers.  Bosses are mostly well done - they have a variety of attack patterns and ways to avoid their attacks.  In probably the only instance I've seen, actually moving into the boss's hitbox can help avoid attacks since many of their attacks hit from the edges of their weapons, and you can avoid some things by being too close.  Combat has issues though.  Attacking is sometimes clunky but always very, very slow for most weapons when attacking from the ground.  You can get animation locked on a lot of things unable to dodge or block in time.  There is a parry mechanic but it's awful - the timing is as always suspect but it also pales in comparison to blocking, as you can also "perfect block" if you block as an attack hits which also staggers the attacker (even bosses!) and allows you to counter similar to parrying.  So blocking is just better.  The "heroic" versions of bosses get really obnoxious with many becoming bullet hell dodgeathons with projectiles flying everywhere and additional foes shooting from the sides.  It's a super derivative game that honestly has zero creativity or innovation whatsoever.  You have every single thing you expect to find in a metroidvania - double jump, air dash, yadda yadda.  Basically every single feature and facet is copied from other better games, including some of the very ugly features that they should NOT be emulating - such as having to follow a guide or else you get the wrong ending or your vendor NPCs in your home base area will randomly and PERMANENTLY die.  Accidentally attacked that NPC?  Well now you can't craft ANYTHING for the rest of the game!  Stop putting this into games.  No one likes it.  It's bad design.  Just stop.  The level design isn't too great either.  Metroidvania level design quality really boils down to how functional are the levels BEFORE you get double jump, and the longer the game goes without the double jump the better.  Somewhere between Symphony of the Night (~40% through) and Super Metroid (~75% of the way through) is best - it should feel like the world has unlocked and backtracking should have newer faster routes unlocked with your ability to move higher.  Sadly this game gives you the double jump extremely early, and the levels are mostly assuming you have it, with most shortcuts being of the "get to the other side of the gate and open it or pull a lever" variety.  What the game does do well is the exploration.  You're quite free to explore in many different directions as you see fit and stumble upon extra bosses and optional areas often.  The game interface is very janky.  The inventory is particularly a mess, with all your items getting thrown together in one giant pool leaving you having to slog through page after page of items with no ability to sort them.  There is a filtering for categories but you have to enable that EVERY time and you're going to this screen often.  Many items don't stack either, leaving you having to constantly clean things up by scrapping extra items - for EXTREMELY minimal rewards.  The map is another serious issue.  The inventory is tab 1 of your menu screen and the map is nonsensically tab 4, and you have to constantly cycle back and forth between them.  Usually you want the map, but often you'll need the inventory, and it is utterly obnoxious swapping.  There should have been a button to open the map, and a different button for the rest of the menus.  Map also has extremely limited functionality.  The fundamental issue is the game is not bad, but you have probably played numerous other games exactly like this that have done every single system better than what is on display here.  If you're satisfied with just another 2D Soulslike, then have at it.  Just don't expect to be blown away.

Сделано с душой, это чувствуется. Боссы иногда отбитые... удар прилетает раньше визуального.... А иногда вообще заходишь, и он умирает, пока ты ещё изучаешь мувмент. Баланс такой… на любителя. Можно случайно прокачаться до имбы, пока просто ищешь куда идти. Навигации почти никакой к сожалению, крутись сам как хочешь, но приятно когда находишь какой-нибудь потайной проход или лут. История вроде как есть, и вроде даже интересная, но чтобы её понять, надо читать, догадываться, собирать по крохам. Кто-то кайфанёт, кто-то, как я, просто будет скипать всё это и идти драться с очередным пиксельным ублюдком с мечом в три экрана. Персонажей можно собирать по-разному, классов семь, и у каждого свой стиль. Приятно, что билд реально влияет на процесс. Снаряги куча, собираешь всё, как хомяк, потому что кто знает, может этот ржавый гвоздь потом нужен будет в жопе мира. Иногда игра глючит к сожалению. Не критично, но типа ты что-то выронил, а оно исчезло. И ты такой.. "окак" Неприятненько. Короче, это как дарк соулс, если бы он был двухмерный, с пиксельной графикой и лёгкой шизой. Местами сыровато, местами кривовато, но играется бодро, держит, не отпускает. С перегибами, но с кайфом.  Крепкий середнячок. На скидке 50-60%, смело брать.

This was an interesting soulslike / metroidvania.  I didn't expect to like it as much as I did, but it was on sale and I'm a glutton for these types of games.  From a time perspective, I've achieved 2 of the 4 endings; I've seen C & D.  Endings A and B are a little tougher to complete, and I think C is considered the standard ending.  It only took me a little over 10 hours to beat the game.  So, from that perspective, it's relatively short and only worth picking up on sale.  Now, from a content perspective, I have a whole lot more that I could do in this game.  There is NG+ with a variety of difficulty increases (normal game is 0 and NG+ goes up to 10).  There are heroic level bosses that increase the challenge of some of the original fights both in level and new move sets.  There are dlc/patch bosses that aren't part of the 'main' game.  So, there is still definitely a lot of content to keep me busy if I decide to keep playing.  The music and story are both really good.  I like the weapon variety and there are a ton of talents to select for different builds.    For how I play games, I very much doubt that I get too far into the weeds on this one like I have with other similar games.  But, I will leave it as a solid recommendation.  The right kind of fan could probably get 50+ hours out of this one.

TLDR - Game is mid.  It's not as good as Hollow Knight, Salt and Sanctuary, Ender Lilies, or Dead Cells. But it's not bad. Nothing really unique either, kind of generic as far as soulsgames go nowadays. It just doesn't really nail anything it does.  The good: Music is ok. Pretty nice at some areas. Oddly funny humor here and there. A decent amount of weapons/builds to try. Death has a somewhat interesting concept.  The not so good: Very early you will notice what kind of skills you'll receive, no surprise and nothing spectacular. The map pointer that shows where you are is not always correct which can be very frustrating. The hitboxes can be a bit weird, not often, but when it happens you certainly notice. Enemies get stuck in places, making them easy targets. Animations/movements are mixed between fluid and sometimes stiff. Voice acting is a hit and miss. Some are good, others are not. No real oomph in sound effects, especially weapons.  I paid 10 euros. Fair price on a sale. I'm not sure I'll bother finishing it.  6/10

This is a decent game, but I felt the balance was off. I got stuck a couple of times and the game does not make any effort to guide you to your main objective. This resulted in me roaming around the entire big map multiple times, and as a result of this my character ended up being over-levelled. Because, while the first half of the game felt easy and had the right amount of challenge, the second half got easier to a point where I started killing bosses on my first attempt before even learning their attack patterns. I literally killed the final boss on my first try - and that is NOT a feeling you want to have playing a "hardcore" game like this- which it's supposed to be.   Yes there is a New Game + that possibly remedies this problem, but come on, this game ain't so epic to warrant replays. I'm not going to play New Game + to experience the game the "right" way. I beat the final boss, the credits rolled, so that's it - I'm uninstalling.   Also, the game takes its story way too seriously, and for a 2D pixel art metroidvania, I really don't care for it. I kept skipping it all because the writing is so mixed anyway - half of it tries to be funny, half of it serious. I did not fancy the story one bit.   That being said it's not a bad game at all - the combat is decent, the levels and art look good. And chances are you'll probably have a better experience with the game. I just felt the complete lack of hand-holding was counter-intuitive to the experience because the game got too easy for my liking - especially with the bosses because my character was over-levelled.  Eventually for me, this was one of those games that wasn't bad at all, but the whole time I was playing it, it made me question if I was wasting my time and if I could be playing something else better.

Death's Gambit Afterlife is straight up one of the best games I think I've ever played, up there with Witcher 3 and Dishonored for me. The smooth satisfying combat, the art style bursting with flavour, the interesting boss fights, the funny moments, and above all the fantastic story. The game starts relatively easy, but difficulty really spikes up roughly half way into the game, especially when you start taking on the *heroic* versions of bosses. The game is called a Souls-like, but honestly it really does play more like an RPG with Souls-like aspects. The game also handles secrets really well, not having too many and not making (most of them) impossible to find. The world size compliments this very well, big enough to make each area feel unique and special, but not so large that re-exploring areas for secrets feels like too big of a hassle. Overall, absolutely loved every bit of it.

Don't let my low playtime fool you, i've beaten this game multiple times.  This is one of the best 2D Soulslike Metroidvanias of all time and it's not even debatable.   The Bad: - It's a bit glitchy here and there, but i've never had something truly gamebreaking.  - Standard Keybinds are whack, but you can reassign them easily. - Some Bosses have some weird hitzones & bs instant attack animations. - Sometimes when you drop an Item, it just vanishes lol. Don't accidentally drop items.   The Mid: - Bosses are pretty easy early on, but they eventually get a lot harder. - Zone layouts can sometimes be a bit confusing even with the Minimap. - Grinding is sometimes required.   The Good:  - Top 5 2D Metroidvania Gameplay ever honestly. It's super fun & addicting.  - Progression is super clean and imo. borderline perfect. - Buildcrafting is really good. You have a LOT of options on how to play your char.  - I like the Story & Writing, but i know that's extremely subjective.  - Actually good Mouse Support for a 2D Metroidvania. Very rare in this Genre. - The Metroidvania Aspect is REALLY FKN GOOD. A lot of Hidden Items, Backtracking, Interconnected Paths. IT'S SO GOOD.  I love this game. It's honestly a really good 8/10 to me.

While I would say this game is closer to a 2D action RPG than a true metroidvania, this game is still absolutely worth a play if you like either genre. I don't know what it was like before the Afterlife update, but I can say that it has very satisfying combat, beautiful visuals, a top-tier soundtrack and a solid story with some great twists, emotional scenes and voice acting that punches above its weight.  That said, the game's not perfect. The story is good overall, but there are clear missteps with certain antagonists ending up pretty much irrelevant to the plot in the end, and an ending that feels disjointed and rushed. Especially because one of the most important bosses in the game story-wise, and a very enjoyable boss fight, is locked behind the Ashes of Vados DLC and isn't integrated into the ending but instead accessed from the post-game arena. Said DLC is also grossly overpriced, being almost half the price of the game itself for an arena with a few different gauntlets, a boss rush, an optional side area that re-uses assets from other parts of the game and the single boss fight.  Despite that, if the game looks interesting to you I would still recommend it. It's a great and memorable game despite its flaws. The game does leave some plot threads open for a future sequel, and I would love to see a sequel be made that improves on what it did well while having a less disjointed, more cohesive story. I would just wait on a sale before purchasing the Deluxe Edition which includes the DLC, or a sale on the DLC itself, because the content in the DLC is not worth the asking price.

Death’s Gambit: Afterlife is a masterfully reworked and expanded version of the original 2018 release—a 2D action RPG that dares to blend punishing Soulslike combat with classic Metroidvania exploration. The game shines brightest, taking full advantage of the platform’s strengths with smooth performance, precise controls, and fast load times. This is the version to play if you want the cleanest and most customizable experience of Sorun’s somber journey through the lands of Siradon.  Combat in Afterlife is tense and deliberate. Every swing, dodge, and parry matters. It’s a dance of attrition where learning enemy patterns is essential, especially during the game’s multi-phase, screen-filling boss battles. Each class feels distinct, and the expanded skill tree system allows for deeper build customization than before. Whether you prefer the ranged finesse of the Acolyte or the heavy-handed brutality of the Sentinel, there's a satisfying rhythm to the combat once mastered.  the responsiveness is a major asset. With keyboard and mouse or controller support, the inputs are snappy and reliable, which is vital in a game where a single mistimed dodge can mean death. Additionally, the game supports higher refresh rates and resolutions, which enhances both the visual clarity and gameplay fluidity.  Siradon is a dark, beautiful world teeming with secrets. The interconnected zones are cleverly laid out, offering meaningful shortcuts and diverse environments that range from crumbling citadels to otherworldly voids. Exploration feels truly rewarding, with hidden bosses, lore items, and new gear tucked into every corner. The Metroidvania mechanics are refined here—new abilities like the air dash and hookshot open up previously inaccessible paths, giving you constant incentives to revisit old areas.  The map has been reworked in Afterlife to be more readable and useful, and players benefit from the added convenience of hotkeys and faster navigation through menus.  Death’s Gambit tells a story about death, loyalty, and identity with surprising emotional weight. You play as Sorun, a soldier bound to Death’s service, slowly unraveling the secrets of immortality and confronting the consequences of it. The game doesn’t spoon-feed its lore—instead, it asks you to piece together the fragments from cryptic dialogue, haunting visions, and visual storytelling.  The clean audio mix and high-definition visuals make it easier to soak in the game’s melancholic atmosphere. The soundtrack is a particular standout: orchestral, haunting, and perfectly timed to the flow of combat and exploration.  Death’s Gambit: Afterlife is visually stunning. The pixel art is intricate, the animations fluid, and the boss designs grotesquely beautiful. The improved lighting and particle effects bring a dynamic edge to scenes of death and combat. The game runs flawlessly on even modest setups, and scaling options allow you to tweak visuals for performance or aesthetics.  There’s also mod potential for players who want to tweak the experience, although the mod scene is still niche.  Death’s Gambit: Afterlife is a rare kind of re-release: one that completely redefines and redeems its source material. The game offers the most refined and flexible way to experience this haunting, high-stakes journey through death and redemption. If you're a fan of Hollow Knight, Dark Souls, or Blasphemous, this deserves a spot in your library.  Pros: -Deep, rewarding combat system -Gorgeous pixel art and atmosphere -Massive content expansion over original -The game runs and plays beautifully -Meaningful build and class variety  Cons: -Some lore elements remain cryptic or underexplained -Occasional difficulty spikes might frustrate newcomers -Backtracking can feel tedious later on  A brooding and brilliant fusion of Metroidvania and Soulslike design, Death’s Gambit: Afterlife is the definitive version of an underappreciated gem. Dark, difficult, and deeply rewarding.  Rating: 9/10

Определённо одна из лучших метроидваний. Топ 3 после Nine sols и Hollow Knight. Визуал, боссы, история (которая и до выхода дополнения была неплоха, как раз в стиле молчаливого Dark souls), и, конечно, музыка (Dark Knight и Moonlight Sniper навсегда в моём сердце).  Без минусов тоже не обошлось. Чего стоит только платформинг, особенно под конец игры в аналоге пути боли из хк. Ну и  пару боссов тоже оставляют желать лучшего. Первый - это Совет, который на героике превращается в ужаснейший буллет хелл. А второй - бесполезный ARGшный космический босс, перематывающий время, моделька которого взята от одного из мобов. Задумка классная, но босс так себе.  Но это все мелочи. По крайней мере проходить эту игру на 100% было очень весело, хоть и муторно в некоторых моментах. Если бы в нг+ давали поднимать максимальный уровень на 150, а не на 50, я бы ещё в ней задержался, но увы, больше здесь делать нечего.

[h3] Одна из худших метроидваний. [/h3] Из плюсов тут только пиксельный арт-дизайн. А из минусов все остальное:   [u] Навыки [/u]. Они полный шлак. 100500 нываков из который для твоего оружия доступно только 3 (последнее вообще под конец открывается) играл через жнеца и не хотел использовать копья и другое оружие на ловкость по причине "так захотел" и в итоге в игре всего 3 косы, одна из которых хрень за 500 эссенций;   [u] Парирования [/u]. Играть через него, это как удариться мизинцем об угол - больно и неприятно (кроме того что тебя хилит после казни врага). Тайминги кал, учить парирование чтобы оправдать один навык в моем выбраном классе туфта;   [u] Дополнительный выбор класса [/u]. Как я уже говорил, играл через жнеца с косой, и тут буквально нет доп класса, который подойдет для тебя. Если выбирать самый банальный класс мечника, то там аж 3 или 4 класса под него заточено. Я в шоке с этого;   [u] Телепорт [/u]. Дают под конец игры, а всю оставшеюся часть ты должен будешь ходить пешком или на лошади скакать туда-сюда, тупо тратить свое время на это душнилово. Тупая задумка если честно;   [u] Инвентарь [/u]. Крайне неудобный. Привыкал к нему всю игру, но так и не смог. Шляпа полнейшая впихивать не отсортированные изначально предметы, где тебе приходиться листать в конец списка чтобы найти то что подобрал. Также нельзя использовать прямо из инвентаря, тебе нужно: 1) экипировать в предметы;  2) использовать; 3) убрать из экипированных предметов. То бишь совершать лишние действия;   [u] Враги [/u].  Под конец они вообще не дают покоя и вообще там босс становиться рядовым противником в одной локации (где-то я такое уже видел). засовывать противников, дамажущих по 1/3 или 1/4 полоски здоровья, как босс, выбор так себе. сложность - ради сложности; В середине игры начал проходить с бессмертием, чтобы не наскучило иначе бы дропнул игру.  Про сюжет: в целом неплох, ни рыба, ни мясо. жаль не дают ачивку если в начале свернуть налево Про юмор: он зачем-то тут есть хоть игра и имеет жанр "темное фэнтези" но честно, он тут не к месту вообще.  [spoiler] Потраченных денег и времени жаль, 5-кратно переваренный кал [/spoiler]

Не игра а помойка, дешманский мобильный аналог элдена в виде платформера, с ужасной боевой системой, с отвратительным музыкальным оформлением, с примитивным сюжетом. Эта игра пытается косить в стиль дакр фентези, но это у неё получается так же, как у байкера с бородой до колен, получилось бы играть золушку в фильмах. Серьёзно, не покупайте эту игру, ДАЖЕ ПО СКИДКЕ В 80-90%, Она даже качественное управление и локализацию не сможет отбить за эту цену, это буквально индюшатина в плохом смысле этого слова. Это та самая игра, где от тебя требуют чтобы ты повернул стик на клавиатуре, это та самая игра, где в меню кнопками ничего делать нельзя, управление только через мышь, НО ТАМ НЕТ КУРСОРА! ЕГО ПРОСТО НЕТ! ОН ДАЖЕ НЕ НЕВИДИМЫЙ! ОН ПРОСТО ОТСУТСТВУЕТ! Это та самая игра, где настройки сбрасываются после каждого выхода из игры. Если у вас есть контроллер, можете пострадать от фигового геймплея и убогой графики (она буквально не соответствует скринам и трейлеру). Игра явно 2\10. Я бы докинул балла 2, если бы она была нормальной хотя бы с технической точки зрения.

优缺点都很明显的游戏 优点：游戏内容丰富，boss战多且精彩，演出优秀。难度方面得益于较长的无敌帧判定，使得通关很容易，不过英雄模式再战还是需要一定熟练度。休闲和挑战都能收获乐趣。只看游戏内容的话完全对的上“好玩”两字 缺点：前期不给传送跑图非常麻烦，骑马速度也就那样；坐椅子换图会有明显黑屏卡顿；意义不明的机翻，比如平a的最后一段能翻成连续终结技，类似错误还很多；部分设定解密不明朗，比如避难所的蜥蜴是个杀手会把别的npc都杀了这个设定，第一次玩不看攻略谁能知道，加上游戏冷门缺乏攻略，很容易就变无头苍蝇；一旦进结局存档就会自动下周目，不能反过来回去搜集或是一档多结局； 总结：玩进去的话这些缺点都不算啥，整体体验线性上升，越后面越好玩

This game feels weirdly nostalgic, so to speak. Yeah that is very strange, it is hard to explain but it is attractive.  To be upfront, the game starts off rather weak: it sort of presents itself as just another average Metroidvania; the storytelling and world-building are not quite engaging; and the so-called "deep RPG elements" are nothing exceptional, if not outright shallow. The game is also brutally unforgiving, it is totally possible to fail to instill that initial momentum to get more casual players hooked.  But then, past a certain point later in the game, all sorts of crazy things start to happen—the grand reveal of the story, the blend of fantasy and sci-fi elements, the fourth-wall-breaking narrative, and so on. The level of mind-♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ reminded me a hella lot of the old-school [i]Might and Magic[/i] and [i]Wizardry[/i], where the story’s development toward the end is always full of surprises, which never failed to catch me off guard.  This game certainly still has a lot of rough edges that could use more polishing—even after the revised Afterlife edition—especially in terms of gameplay. That said, it manages to bring to the table its own merits: a rich story, beautiful music, and tasteful wit and humour. There is a very specific charm in it.

Death's Gambit is... good! I had an enjoyable enough time with it. It's more souls than metroidvania (though it definitely is the latter) but in an unusual twist, there's a class that's, like, fast-paced and actually fun from the getgo. The game says it's for "experts" which is funny because I'm positive I would've had a much harder time playing as any other class. Mixture of dodge-friendly build and decent usage of shield/shield countering and just going ham with daggers lead to having a pretty good time throughout.  It's also gorgeous to look at and mostly gorgeous in motion (though some of the animations feel very, uhh, uncanny? Like it's a pixel game but stuff just, rotates, a lot, it isn't animated rotating. Which would be more work, I understand, but, it stands out, haha. It's pretty colorful for the kind of dark, moody game it is - and the game's way goofier and funnier than you'd expect looking from the outside - and I like all that! The protagonist is, y'know, a character. All good stuff.  The main issue I have is that some of the game just feels... unpolished? Jank? In a way I can't quite describe but it lacked the tightness of other souls metroidvanias I've played like blasphemous and nine sols. It's... hard to put into words but fairly obvious when playing it. The game isn't especially hard despite that, I beat it in some 11.5 hours and spent a few more poking at new game plus before deciding to put it down since the heroic bosses seemed... way overtuned for what I felt like dealing with, for the most part, and I'd need to fight them to, y'know, beat the game for real, so.  If there's anything to hold against this game it's that it takes. So much, from the souls games. Fire-breathing phoenix that guards a path. Boss that revives itself if you don't kill a few monsters before it. NPC who kills other NPCs in the sanctuary. Uber-specific beats that are, like. More copying than straight homage, it feels like. The story is, fine, the big twist at the end felt kinda predictable but there were some nice scenes even if I never felt suuuuper strongly about any of it or the world lore. And you're not really going to get the whole story on a single playthrough, either, you're REALLY encouraged to NG+ at least once to see everything. Probably more times, but, I stopped not long after my first playthrough, so.  It's also kinda... annoying? That you don't unlock fast travel until the very very end of the game. Like, you do *get* it but too late for it to be useful for anything other than going back for full completion, which... this game didn't spark the completionist urge in me, so. It also feels like after a point your healing feathers become... basically useless as they heal less than a single hit from a boss does, even the stronger ones, and there's no way to upgrade them. Usually there's some kind of scaling for your heals in this kind of game but, here? Nope.  I think the end result is that the game is way more ambitious than it has the chops to actually live up to, but even playing it fairly casually and being satisfied with seeing An Ending, it was a fun enough experience. It's a game that I've known of for ages but got scared off from by the original launch being kinda shaky, but it was on sale and I was curious enough to check it out, and... yeah, I can't say I'm *disappointed* but I wouldn't say I'm particularly over the moon for it, either. It's Good but it's missing something to make it great. Still worth a play if you're a fan of 2D soulslikes, though.

Probabilmente sarò io il problema, ma proprio non riesco a mandare giù certe cose. Dopo questo titolo, credo proprio che non acquisterò più alcun gioco di cui non né sono convinto. Appena nutro dei dubbi, da adesso in poi ascolterò le mie sensazioni ed eviterò l'acquisto perché quasi sempre poi finisco col droppare il gioco. La prima cosa che anzitutto di questo gioco non mi ha mai convinto sul serio è la direzione artistica che c'è dietro la trama, perché l'idea è ok e l'introduzione molto d'impatto e lascia ben sperare. Poi, appena inizi il gioco ti mette solo dei dialoghi qui e lì ogni tanto e fine. Sembra che poi della storia se né siano completamente scordati, non accompagnandoti più durante la progressione e rendendo il gioco molto staccato. Poi il level design, una delle cose che più ho odiato. Mappe spiaccicate a caso senza alcuna cura, tutto molto randomico e poco vivo, come se nulla prendesse realmente vita e fosse decontestualizzato, ti fanno esplorare le zone in maniera molto disordinata e poco chiara, spesso i mob sono messi lì a caso e non c'è un reale livello di difficoltà, come se fosse quasi un sandbox.  La progressione del personaggio non parliamone, pochissimi upgrade ed albero skill molto limitato, comparto armi insufficienti.. ho fatto il 50 superato, quasi 50% mappa esplorata e ho trovato 1 SOLA ARMA per la mia classe, ma scherziamo? Pochi stimoli e abilità super ripetitive e scialbe. Anche qui.. mi piazzi un genere souls ma è tutto davvero molto fiacco. Per non parlare dell'esplorazione di mappa, mi fai mettere i segnalini e me né limiti ad un paio? Non metterli proprio a sto punto... scelta davvero strana, preferivo non averla proprio la mappa se poi lasci il lavoro a metà. Combattimento che tra l'altro presenta diversi bug, sia di interazioni che di movimenti. Alcune fight le ho vinte praticamente abusando di alcuni bug perché altrimenti impossibili da fare in maniera sana. Spesso le fight sono per lo più lunghe anziché difficili ed impegnative, riuscivo a vincere senza problemi contro qualunque boss semplicemente diventava una tortura ucciderli perché il combattimento era molto lento.  Ero abbastanza livellato ma ci voleva parecchio tempo affinché la barra scendesse, e questo mascherava dei ritmi all'apparenza frenetici. Per non parlare dell'intera esperienza che si ha nell'affrontare certi boss, completamente sbilanciati e frustranti. O livelli e diventi OP oppure è tutto completamente RNG. Boss che spesso spamma abilità e copre l'intera mappa di combattimento, dovendo stare per gran parte del tempo a perma dodgiare, hai pochissimo spazio per attaccare e quando lo fai ti becchi le sberle in faccia che ti levano mezza barra di vita. Io onestamente l'ho trovato poco focussato questo titolo, ok l'avrò giocato per circa 5h ma fidatevi che di progressi li ho fatti e non c'è bisogno di finirlo un gioco per capire se diverte o meno, 50% di mappa esplorata direi è sufficiente per capire che non fa per me. E dire che ho finito ed apprezzato un gioco similissimo per struttura, ma a mio modo di vedere molto ben fatto e strutturato con più accuratezza ed impegno, ossia Salt and Sanctuary. Credo che questo gioco sia adatto a chi è puro amante dell'immersione in esplorazione/combattimenti continui senza una solida e accurata lavorazione del contorno. E questo per me è uno di quei giochi.

I finished Death Gambit Afterlife a few years ago, but after stumbling upon some videos, I thought writing a review would be a good way to show my appreciation for it.  When I started playing it before the Afterlife rework, I dropped it at the Bulwark (second boss of the game). I really didn't enjoy it and felt it was sluggish and way too hard for nothing. However, with the numerous changes of Afterlife, I had a much better time and went up until the end.  This game is my absolute favorite story wise, I keep getting teary-eyed when rewatching some scenes with Death, the way the story is written along with interactions is genuinely extremely good. Although I'd say it takes until at least the mid-point to third half of the game for the story to go from "decent" to "amazing". You can see the various inspirations the devs had from other famous indie games, which I obviously won't mention to avoid spoiling the "surprise". The ending itself is among my most cherished experiences, I've recommended the game many times because of that last half that just blew me out the water the first time.  Gameplay wise, I think it's fine. However, making some choices (like choosing the Acolyte of Death class at the start) are really important, it makes the game less of a hassle as some bosses can be really unforgiving. I personally didn't try out the other weapons, the scythe is well-rounded so I always went with that one.  NG+ on heroic bosses is actually not fun because of how you don't really stand a chance, felt more like a slap in the face than a challenge, but the base game difficulty is well-thought-out.  As for the music, I've been so surprised of how good it is, the main leitmotif is something that stuck with me through the journey. Be it from bosses or just certain biomes, I'm always remembering them fondly, this is not your average OST that doesn't stand out.  An example : https://youtu.be/e4WURvWH1kI?si=ybluG68zmKP4WSI2  I absolutely recommend this game if you enjoy challenging games and a story that has one of the most beautiful souls I've ever seen. Thank you for making this game  Death's Gambit 2 will probably never happen, but I'm happy even if it ends this way.

Безусловно, это одна из лучших игры в которую я играл, визуал, музыка, геймплей и сюжет, все на высоте. Да у игры есть свой огрехи, как с тем же переводом, но я готов более чем закрыть на это глаза, чего стоить душещипательный сюжет от которого любой пусти скупую скупую мужскую слезу, и куча Босов со своим лором, и их героическими версиями. В целом всем рекомендую.

Sinceramente este juego lo recibí como regalo por mi cumpleaños y no tenia ni idea de la existencia de este juego pero tras habérmelo pasado con el final verdadero en NG+1 me hubiera gustado haberlo conocido de antes por que es maravilloso. La gente dice que se inspira en dark souls pero la verdad que no lo sentí así, solo note pequeñas cosas o referencias pero nada idéntico o muy parecido. De la historia no quiero destripar nada pero es muy bonita y original, los jefes increíbles y su apartado artístico y banda sonora muy memorable. La jugabilidad buena y sobre todo rápida y frenética (puede que al principio no tanto pero conforme avanzas se vuelve una más frenetica). Una pena que no se hable ni se conozca mucho este juego por que es muy bueno y sobre todo al precio que tiene recomenado al 100%.

Abrí este juego por accidente, pero me llevé una gran y hermosa sorpresa por lo hermoso que es. 🥺  Nunca antes había probado un juego de este tipo, me animaron a continuarlo cuando lo abrí sin querer, aunque jamás esperé adquirir una vivencia así de maravillosa.   Al inicio claramente no sabía qué hacer, estaba bastante perdida, sin saber a dónde ir, pero conforme pasó el tiempo, me adapté a la jugabilidad e intenté seguir adelante. Cometí un error tras otro conforme avanzaba y en ocasiones me frustraba cuando enfrentaba a ciertos jefes.   A pesar de ello, me emocionaba muchísimo la historia y quería seguir sin importar nada más. Toda la trama es simplemente una joya, puedes sentirte inmerso en lo que ocurre a lo largo del recorrido en el juego y empatizar con Sorun y los demás, dejándote un recuerdo lleno de calidez en el corazón.   Lo que al inicio fue un simple error se convirtió en mi juego preferido por mucho. Fue tan agradable que quise seguir y terminarlo hasta NG+20 solo por querer vivir la misma experiencia una y otra vez, observando los cambios que se presentaban en cada incremento de dificultad.   Sin duda recomiendo este juego, es lo mejor que me ha pasado en la vida. ♡ Death's Gambit fue un dulce compañero para mí que nunca olvidaré, tanto que lo volvería a jugar otra vez.

meta元素类银巅峰剧情神作——亡灵诡计 如果说此前玩过的类银超绝剧情设计给我带来的感觉是震撼，那么这部作品最后的BOSS——塔拉慕斯的演出已经让我惊愕。 剧情以一个自称“死神”的人，用已故的主人公索伦的灵魂与你签订契约为引，没错，就是屏幕前的玩家——你，揭露了一个因为王权对永生之源的贪婪而引发的世纪血战背后的真相。经典的救世题材，以主人公寻亲并找寻永生之源为主线缓缓展开。  剧情设计上：并没有加入太多的伏笔，直截了当的单线叙事，细致剧情可以从与几位主要NPC的对话和死亡后死神的某些“抱怨”中窥探一二，强有力的叙事在前期可能不如游戏性更吸引人，但到了真正与塔拉慕斯会面之后，就会让玩家有重新回顾剧情的打算。此外与类银很少关联的特殊的meta的大胆加入也增强了剧情代入感，玩家在最后结局的参与感指数型增长。并且最终无限血与塔拉慕斯的战斗和结局群战塔拉慕斯的设计也都很独特大胆，杂货商用热气球带蜥蜴哥和长枪姐回来时真的给我感动到了。  游戏性上：这点要特别注意，此游戏对近战玩家非常非常不友好，甚至于刁难，所以不想坐牢纯粹体验游戏的玩家开局选职建议直接巫师起手，超强增益buff、高爆发输出、远程灵活走位都很容易上手，绝对选不了吃亏，选不了上当。单独提一点游戏主角机动性已经很强了，二段跳、空冲、俯冲、无限跳已经是类银里很难同时搭配上的了，并且空冲很丝滑，搭配一个特殊装备加长冲刺距离更是丝滑不少，就是起跳手感有些重。当然这也并不是说近战就没法玩，近战主要推荐长剑、巨剑一类，所谓一寸长一寸强，我就是开局为了攻速能有所优势选了狂战士，但是这个鬼斧头伤害真的难蚌，而且近战容错特别低，体力条消耗对比大剑也并不少，索性后期换了长剑和巨剑来打。近战想打得舒服完美盾反是必须要掌握的，再搭配一下闪避走位可以拉高很多容错，前期可能并不会影响很多，但到了后期打一些英雄模式的BOSS就会有所体会。盾反的练习建议用英雄模式的凤凰骑士和无尽来练，熟练之后依赖性很强而且容错也高。武器推荐长剑里的灼烧之刃和巨剑里的黑暗骑士的巨剑，这两个升级拉高伤害绝对不亏，并且火属性和暗属性法伤在游戏里都是万金油，打冰原领主和大星术师就能体验到了。前面说游戏对近战玩家有所刁难还有一点，游戏里有完全物防的小怪，需要搭配攻击性异能才能击杀。跳跳乐的话不多说，加点体力值卖血都能莽过。（ps：1.中央庇护所存档点旁边NPC冥想不要按键，之后可以返还天赋点重点天赋 2.游戏内有两种宝箱，一种金色的不用羽毛回血开，一种暗紫色的要追着砍爆开 3.建议前期在杂货商那多备乌鸦羽毛，回城真的很方便 4.传送要在很后期才能得到，前期骑马跑图能快一些 5.进入游戏角色不能动看一下输入法是否是英文）  游戏引导：几乎没有，但好在给了目标地点，并且地图有未探索区域提示，地上区域地图有类银基础还是很好探索的，但地下赛博基地就有点绕了，可以看攻略。  玩法设计上：及其贴合魂类游戏的天赋、装备、技能系统，养成系的战斗模式很有味道。并且几乎没有的死亡惩罚（羽毛掉落不会消失）也能减少挫败感，否则近战玩家真的很难玩下去了。虽然没有BOSS连战，但是能在地图内多次挑战进阶级BOSS也是增加了游戏可玩性，并且掉落高阶装备也是会有正反馈的，多周目、自选限制的流程挑战模式也都有，增加了多次游玩体验。  BOSS设计上：每个BOSS都有独特的设计，并且在地形、招式上也都可以看出对人物背景的思考，不知道法师玩家体验如何，但是近战绝对会对大多数BOSS都有很深刻的印象。除可盾反的近战外，大多是弹幕、陷阱、地形杀、连招、半屏斩形式，需要灵活走位躲避，难度很高。数值并不是很夸张，打同等级BOSS可以抗四下以上，配合羽毛回血多磨几次是可以战胜的。BOSS击杀后掉落特殊武器、特殊材料、天赋点等正反馈很强。塔拉慕斯的两次BOSS演出就不再多说，真的非常用心，非常精致。  画面与音乐：像素画风，游戏场景稍弱于渎神，但角色立绘已经不相上下。存档和地图过渡会有黑屏，可以提升一下。音乐很恢宏，几乎都是宿命感特别强的BGM，尤其主题曲，听下来会感觉很有故事深度。  dlc已入库，马上体验。

[b]Death's Gambit: Afterlife[/b] é uma daquelas joias raras que costuma surgir de tempos em tempos. O combate é desafiador, porém agradável. Há grande variedade de armas, equipamentos, classes e árvores de talentos. A exploração é muito gratificante, repleta de segredos, com chefes ocultos e caminhos não lineares que incentivam a curiosidade.  O mundo de [b]Siradon[/b] é belamente sombrio, ganhando vida com uma trilha sonora fantástica e uma arte em pixel deslumbrante. A história mergulha em temas como morte, imortalidade e propósito. A história é enriquecida por personagens marcantes e escolhas significativas que levam a múltiplos finais.  Se você curte jogos como [b]Hollow Knight[/b] ou [b]Blasphemous[/b], dê uma chance a esse jogo. Facilmente um dos melhores [i]Metroidvanias[/i] independentes que existem. Altamente recomendado!  [b]P.S.:[/b] Recomendo que você salve o jogo assim que derrotar a boss [b]Endless[/b]. Depois disso, vá até a pasta "C/users/%username%/AppData/Local/deathsgambit397" e faça uma cópia da pasta "deaths_gambit0" (que corresponde ao slot 1 dos seus savegames) e renomeie essa cópia para "deaths_gambit1" (que corresponde ao slot 2). [i]Não interaja com a [b]Fonte da Imortalidade[/b] antes de salvar e fazer essa cópia[/i]. Isso é importante porque, logo após derrotar a [b]Endless[/b], você precisará tomar uma decisão que determinará se você verá o final A ([b]Death’s Gambit[/b]) ou o final B ([b]Thalamo’s Gambit[/b]) do jogo. Essa é uma boa forma de experimentar os dois finais sem ter que começar tudo do zero.

As an example you can literally side with the villain in the end. I would say this game has made it as my Number 2 Metroidvania of all time.

7,5/10.  It's a great game. The story is touching, the artstyle and music are beautiful, the mechanics and gameplay are fun, and the map is vast to explore.   However, there's a big problem concerning it's difficulty scaling system. There are points where the game simply doesn't allow you to go any further level/equipment wise - and, with that, you just can't defeat some enemies. It's just nuts that it has a monstruous NG+20 (Yes, NewGame Plus TWENTY, for god's sake). It's absolutely unreal, the NG+1 already requires your virginity back to fully beat it...  That's my only complaint. Great 2D soullike-metroidvania, it deserves your attention!

中盤まで我慢してやりましたが、すいません、つまらなかったです。 何故か、こういった海外産のメトロイドヴァニアはほぼ必ずと言っていいほど、翻訳ローカライズが壊滅的で、本作もご多分に漏れず、緑のピッコロ大魔王の偽物みたいな主人公や、その他の登場人物全て含めて、何を言っているのかわかりません。こういう難しい、というか複雑な言い回しをするのが英語なのか？ってくらいに、グーグル翻訳以下の内容です。 　アクション面も非常にモッタリした動きで、攻撃時に一歩踏み出しての攻撃モーションとなり、的に近づいて攻撃すると、ほぼ確実にスカります。そのうえ、あまりにもっさりとして振り向きも遅くイライラするレベルです。移動も、ほぼ使いどころのないポータルのせいで、ほとんど全マップを徒歩で移動する感じで、とにかくストレスのたまるゲームでした。中盤のボスで、某スーパーマリオ３のような、傾く島での亀の甲羅の蹴り出しアクションのようなボス戦で、イライラもMaxです。このモッサリ感で、その動きはない。ほんとなめてんのか、ってくらい動きません。セールでほぼ半額で買いましたが、8割オフでやっと我慢できるくらいのレベルです。

This one was kind of a mixed bag.  Its visuals and music were pretty solid, but the story is kind of all over the place.  While nothing is really done poorly, I wasn't a big fan of some of the difficulty spikes here, and there are some mechanics that aren't really explained well to you.  The story is pretty standard at first, but quickly gets pretty meta.  I still didn't LOVE it, but it did get more interesting as time went on.    A lot of metroidvania games I've been playing lately have Dark Souls influences, but this one more heavily leans into that than a lot of others I've played.  There's a lot of tools at your disposal, but again, I wish the game did a better job of explaining some of these to you.  Not a dealbreaker, but as a result, I feel I wasn't encouraged to experiment as I think the game wanted me to.  Some fights are incredibly frustrating and unfair-feeling (especially some of the heroic versions of fights), but admittedly, when you finally get through them, it makes it all the more satisfying.  Overall, I wasn't expecting much from this game, but it managed to surprise me in a few ways.  It wasn't one of my favorites, but I'm definitely glad I gave this a go.

Recenzja gry Deaths Gambit: Afterlife  Deaths Gambit: Afterlife to gra akcji RPG z mocnymi wpływami tzw. gatunku Soulslike. Akcja rozgrywki osadzona jest w mrocznym, pełnym tajemnic świecie, w którym śmierć i odkupienie splatają się w niezwykle klimatyczną opowieść. Gracz wciela się w postać wojownika, który staje na granicy życia i śmierci, przemierzając niebezpieczne lokacje, walcząc z potężnymi przeciwnikami oraz bossami, a każda konfrontacja wymaga nie tylko precyzyjnej zręczności, ale również strategicznego planowania.   Fabuła i Klimat Gra oferuje głęboką, choć momentami ponurą narrację, w której każdy zakątek świata zdaje się kryć własne sekrety. Fabuła osadzona jest w uniwersum, gdzie granica między życiem a śmiercią jest bardzo cienka, a postacie często borykają się z własnymi demonami. Klimat gry przykuwa od pierwszych chwil, dzięki starannie wykreowanej oprawie graficznej oraz muzyce, która potęguje atmosferę nieustannego zagrożenia.   Mechanika Rozgrywki Podstawą rozgrywki jest wymagający system walki inspirowany klasykami gatunku Soulslike. Kluczowymi elementami są: - Precyzyjne Starcia: Każdy pojedynek wymaga dokładnego timingu – odpowiednich uników, bloków i kontrataków. - Rozwój Postaci: Gracze mają możliwość rozbudowywania umiejętności i dostosowywania ekwipunku do własnego stylu gry, co dodaje głębi strategicznej. - Satysfakcjonujące Bossy: Walki z bossami to prawdziwe testy umiejętności, gdzie każde zwycięstwo daje ogromną satysfakcję. - Atmosfera Ryzyka: Zarządzanie energią, regeneracja oraz wymagające starcia sprawiają, że każda potyczka staje się wyzwaniem, a postępy – wyjątkowo nagradzające.  Moje Odbiór i Rekomendacja Chociaż gra Deaths Gambit: Afterlife zachwyca swoim klimatem, głębią fabularną i precyzyjnie zaprojektowaną mechaniką, muszę przyznać, że to nie jest tytuł dla mnie osobiście. Mimo iż doceniam jej walory i wiem, że jest to wspiana produkcja, osobiście nie potrafię zmotywować się do ukończenia jej do końca. Niemniej jednak, głęboko w sercu uważam, że gra ta stanowi prawdziwą perełkę wśród wymagających produkcji i gorąco polecam ją każdemu, kto ceni sobie intensywne emocje oraz satysfakcję płynącą z pokonywania trudnych wyzwań.  Podsumowując, Deaths Gambit: Afterlife to tytuł, który choć może nie być dla każdego, oferuje niezapomniane doświadczenia i stanowi obowiązkową pozycję dla fanów gatunku. Jeśli szukasz gry, która wystawi twoje umiejętności na próbę, a jednocześnie wciągnie cię swoją mroczną, tajemniczą atmosferą  ta produkcja jest zdecydowanie warta uwagi.

Another tired take on “souls-like” combat mixed with poor graphical decisions.  The gameplay is extremely sluggish and doesn't have the same snappy responsiveness that metrovania games should have.  Movement feels lumbering and clumsy.  The gameplay designers have a long way to go in understanding what makes a marriage of metroidvania and souls work.  The animation team really phoned it in with the animations as well.  The walk cycles on the horse and the main character is lifeless and stiff.  Which is a problem as you have to look at your character for the entire game.  They really should have spent more time making the animations more unique and fun.  Also the stiffness really sticks out when the horses head moves.  The head is lazily rotated by the animators instead of redrawing the pixels which gives an ugly puppet like feel.     Also the color choices in this game is… interesting.  Someone please tell the designers and the art director that the enemies shouldnt be the same color palette as the background.  Its impossible to see the enemies until you get hit because of how everything just blends together in a dark neon blue color vomit.    I was drawn in by the pretty painted artwork at first but ended up returning the game after two hours.

GUYS (and gals) AM I INSANE?? Or you've like never played a decent metroidvania, a decent soulslike, a decent game at all???? You've never been told a good story with sufficient execution?? You've never experienced a piece of media that respects you and your time??  Death's Gambit: Afterlife fails in almost every field it touches.  -- it's soulslike combat is trivialised, you either block/dodge like every hit (becouse a) enemies deal no contact damage which means you can just walk behind them b) EVERY shield blocks (i think) EVERY type of hit and stamina regenerate so fast that the enemy won't even hit you again in time), or die under storm of badly communicated attacks;  -- yeah, decent souls games also have simple moveset but they have thoughtful designed and placed enemies. here enemies seems to be placed randomly and there're like four types of them in the entire game?? And it's not the fault of being 2D, see Salt and Sanctuary for great combat and enemies;  -- soulslikes also have good bosses with movesets you have to learn and in dg:a like 80% of the bosses I encountered are just spaming attack button with occasional shield button. I think I beat 15/16 bosses and I finished the whole game in 10 hours which, at least for me, is so low boss fights / playtime ratio lol (I've beaten only the first boss on heroic and it wasn't a big deal but people say they're unbalanced the other way so anyway there's no fun);  -- metroidvania elements have to be one of the worst I've ever seen. You get so unique abilities as a) double jump b) air dash c) crash down d) uhhhhhhhhh infinite jump e) emughtf infinite air dash, and CAN YOU IMAGINE they didn't even bother to give them like a PROPER NAMES?? they're just refered to as as above, you can even discuss them with one of the npcs and he's just like "yeah, you know more about infinite double jump, huh?", that's insane to me;  -- the game has lots of backtracking, gives you almost no hint where to travel to and practically doesn't have a fast travel system. It's not necesarry a bad thing (first example, Hollow Knight does that in an amazing way) but dg:a has so boring and unmemorable areas that finding your next objective is truly a chore;  -- a map should somehow solve the previous problem but dg:a's map is just awful, it's super chaotic in its presentation, icons are basically indistinguishible, you either need to zoom it in so much so you can't see more than two rooms or zoom it out so you can't see locations' names or narrower corridors. You can put your own icons on the map but there's no way to customize them and also good luck doing it on a controller where the coursor moves so ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ fast and there's no way to change it;  -- you also collect literal tons of useless items and other ♥♥♥♥ I stopped caring about after like half an hour. all of that -- weapons and armor, different healing items, consumables, lore items (probably) and whatever else -- all of them goes to one tab in your inventory so good luck finding it lol there's a button to sort items but in which way?? Who knows, for me it looked like that were just five random orders so good luck finding it anyway lol all the other menus is also unintuitive and confusing but I won't discuss every single one of them;  -- upgrades you get -- like the ones you spend talent points on -- add so little to the gameplay. Seriously, I couldn't find one single ability I was looking forward to get, not a single one to turn out to be gamechanging or even slightly useful. Like, you get 10% less damage whenever you heal, ok? Should I be happy about it?, I guess so idk. You could beat this game without realising this system exist and literally nothing would be different about your experience;  -- the lore seems kinda cool.... I can't tell because all of these characters talks. like. all. the. time. You die? Here's the cutscene. You meet two random npcs in the severs? Well, here's their dialogue, it's their sole pourpose. You die to a boss? Let's hear what they wants to tell you (every single time). You've beaten the boss? Let's hear what they wants to tell you:) And all those things are so dull, uninspired and unengaging... I stopped caring about the (simple and generic, I suppose) story after the third time someone interrupted the gameplay and I LOVE playing games for their stories. I really can't think of another example of a media when I felt like the writers wanted me not to care about what they've written lol;  -- and so on, and so on... Really, almost every aspect of Death's Gambit is awful, and it's even after the bit free Afterlife expansion (shout out for the devs for doing it tbh, they could've left the game in even worst condition). The only aspect of the game I'd consider good or even great are pixelart textures, these are really excepcional. Soundtrack is also ok I guess (and I think it's even recorded with a real orchestra, damn) but lacks even single memorable melody and the soundtrack often doesn't match the atmosphere of the location it plays in lol.  tl;dr just play Salt and Sanctuary, it does many similar things but just executes them so much better.

It's pretty decent but nothing special. The combat is fine, there's some depth and build variety, but it felt really unsatisfying to me because of the sound and visual effects.  The boss designs are quite good visually, but mechanically they weren’t anything special and felt way too easy. The later bosses are more interesting, though, and there’s one encounter that’s actually great, which I won’t spoil. The real final boss looks great and seems like a fun, challenging fight, but I don’t really care enough to go for it.  The story didn’t do much for me either. In Souls games, the hopelessness and mystery make me want to explore every corner for lore, but this story was so boring. To be fair, I started zoning out at around 70% because I couldn’t handle the mind-numbing conversations anymore, so maybe it got interesting after that. In Hollow Knight, there’s barely any story, and I love that, but the level design and atmosphere there are way more intriguing.  Visually, the game looks pretty good, and the art direction is solid, but some areas, like the futuristic section, look like ♥♥♥♥. The voice acting is really bad, and the music is even worse. It felt like I was listening to some royalty-free fantasy track they grabbed from a cheap bundle. The boss music is a little better, but not by much.  Overall, I can't say I disliked it, and I honestly enjoyed most of my time with it.

This is a good game, not sure why the current reviews are mixed but I think I know why lets start off with the good: The depth of this game is pretty massive, multiple weapon types and bosses/enemies. However I feel like It is a huge ask to make someone do new game plus after one playthrough, since pretty much everything can be explored in one. The combat mechanics are fun however I feel like the perfect block/ parry is broken, in which it will completely work in your favor in some fights and others will get you killed, even with perfect timing. I liked the class system and that the game supports multiple playstyles, its almost like its trying to be a 2d Elden ring , with optional side quests and different build load outs. But as I said before I just feel like the map is a little small for all of that, like it doesn't have the length to support multiple play throughs with multiple characters. It was fun for what it was and worked well on the steam deck, and at a discount im going to recommend it, I would probably give it a solid 7.5/10, since there was a lot of aspects I liked. Just remember there is some Quality of life things that make things worse. Like run backs for bosses, with enemies that can kill you on the way there. Also you LOSE healing potions every time you die, you can get them back via paying EXP, but why? Like seriously you are going to have me fight this hard boss , and make it harder each time because I have less healing, like give me a break so obvious that its difficult just for the sake of difficult and not actually tweaking the game paly to be challenging. Thats not to say the boss fights are not interesting though, certainly get creative on the 2d plane. ITs just that there is  certain things in this game that make you say "why"

Una piccola perla: imperfetta e grezza, ma di grande valore. Un metrodivania dalla natura soulslike che fa dell'esplorazione e del combattimento i suoi punti forti: nulla di trascendentale o mai visto, ma che funziona liscio come l'olio all'interno del contesto di gioco. Le premesse, cupe e che ruotano attorno al concetto di immortalità desiderata e della sua disperata ricerca, lasciano comunque spazio a una generosa dose di ironia, con alcuni personaggi e situazioni che senza dubbio vi strapperanno più di un sorriso, prima di rigettarvi in cupi cunicoli che hanno il solo scopo di intimidirvi. Da menzionare anche i boss, non originalissimi o complicati, ma realizzati con cura, merito della deliziosa pixel art che muove il tutto. Nonostante gli anni sulle spalle, adesso capisco perché DGA veniva spesso citato come uno di quei metroidvania che val la pena di giocare.  Cheers...a Morte sous chef.

Death's Gambit: Afterlife is a 2D action RPG that blends challenging combat, Metroidvania exploration, & a dark fantasy world. It's an enhanced & expanded version of the original Death's Gambit, addressing many of its criticisms & offering a more polished & complete experience. You play as Sorun, a soldier resurrected by Death, tasked with destroying the source of immortality in a medieval world.  The game's pixel art style is beautiful & detailed, creating a grim & atmospheric world. The environments are varied & interconnected, encouraging exploration & backtracking. The character & enemy designs are also well-done, with some truly memorable bosses.  The combat is challenging & demanding, requiring precise timing & strategic use of your abilities. You can choose from a variety of classes, each with their own unique playstyles & weapons. The combat system has been significantly improved in Afterlife, with more responsive controls & a greater emphasis on player skill.  One of the biggest improvements in Afterlife is the expanded world & story. New areas, bosses, & storylines have been added, providing a much richer & more complete narrative experience. The game also features multiple endings, adding to its replayability.  However, Death's Gambit: Afterlife is still a challenging game, & some players might find the difficulty frustrating. The story, while improved, can still be a bit cryptic at times.  **Pros:**  *   Beautiful pixel art style & atmospheric world! 🎨 *   Challenging & rewarding combat! ⚔️ *   Expanded world & story with new areas & bosses! 🗺️ *   Improved gameplay mechanics & controls! 🎮 *   Variety of classes & playstyles! 👤  **Cons:**  *   Can be very difficult & unforgiving! 😭 *   Story can still be a bit cryptic! 🤔  **Verdict:** 8/10. Death's Gambit: Afterlife is a significant improvement over the original game, offering a more polished & complete experience. Its challenging combat, beautiful art style, & expanded world make it a worthwhile experience for fans of action RPGs & Metroidvanias. While its difficulty might not appeal to everyone, those who are looking for a challenging & rewarding experience will find a lot to appreciate. It's a testament to how much a game can improve with dedicated post-release support. ✨  ***  **Disclaimer:** These reviews are based on my personal experience & opinions. Your mileage may vary. Death’s Gambit is a by-the-numbers metroidvania. It hardly does anything new, and nothing really stands out.  The aspect that most embodies this is the story. It tries to be funny, it tries to be serious, and it tries to subvert your expectations. All attempts fall flat. I could not get invested in any character or plotline. Any joke that came out felt out of place and unfunny, and at the end i was wishing all the characters would just be quiet.  Gameplay is a generic metroidvania most of the time. The map hardly feels interesting and exploring is boring due to simple map design and the metroidvania standard abilities (dash, double jump, ground pound). Environments don’t stand out at all, especially paired with the music which never matches the environment that you’re in.   The only thing that this game has going for it is the bosses. They are alright the first time around, but then you’re allowed (and required for the true ending) to have a rematch with them. These rematches are much harder versions of the fights with more attacks and are more fun than the first attempt. The thing is though, if you want to be optimally prepared for these fights, you have to do a ♥♥♥♥ ton of grinding. I reached the level cap after clearing the main story and wanted to do the remaining rematches, but I hit a brick wall. Farming to get the best gear would literally take hours. It would have been shorter, but a critical NPC was killed by an assassin I was supposed to kill. The game said to me, “♥♥♥♥ you, you didn’t save the merchant. Go farm.” and I say back “♥♥♥♥ that ♥♥♥♥, I’m going to play a better game.”  There are more interesting metroidvanias out there that are more worth your time.

I am learning one should really only buy the 3D souls games FROM the source, but I trust in these indie 2D souls-likes. I have found some rough gems among the search action enjoyers, and with Death's Gambit we have another.  The combat depth, mostly the various weapon abilities, were a blast to mess around with in an attempt to create a build. I wish these synergistic options extended into the armor and skill trees more. It kind of looked like other weapons had some support in the trees, but I went spear in my main play through. I mostly found myself spending my talent points in the optional advanced tree, which provide general buffs. The magic was also great with this ability system. You create and combine different elemental orbs together for various effects. I love when a game actually has you put some components together to cast a spell, not just press button to shoot missile.  The optional heroic fights add to the non-linear nature of Death's Gambit, and were also just really fun. The heroic versions of bosses have similar but expanded movesets. Thankfully not just more damage and health. A few of the heroic fights even give you a surprising upgrade after beating them. I wish that was always the case, but all in all this is a unique way to introduce optional challenging fights, and even more so with game changing rewards.  Lastly, a handful of complaints. The map sucks. It's non-gridded and shows you the entire area right when you walk in which makes it hard to know where you haven't been. The game can't seem to run higher than 60fps and is slightly blurry when moving. I got used to it, but it's not ideal, especially when the pixel art is this good. "Ending A" was fun to set up, but not so fun to execute. Running around areas I have already been, watching emotional cutscenes for characters, and answering questions to see if I paid attention to the game are not why I am playing this game so they should not be the bulk of the ending. I am here for the exploration and boss fights. Speaking of boss fights, [spoiler]Ashe's[/spoiler] fight is so awkwardly left in the game instead of leading up to [spoiler]Thalamus[/spoiler]. Something that was very much needed as the last part of "Ending A" is quite messy in general. The build up to, towering leveling before, and fight with [spoiler]Endless[/spoiler], the normal ending, feels much much better in comparison to the extended ending I chose to go for.  I know I left that off on kind of a negative note, but when I have this much to say about a game it means I love it and I want it to be all that it can be <3  8 / 10

este juego es como un ogro tiene capas, yo jugué el juego hace muchos años, y me gusto mucho, el juego se actualizo y planteó el juego de forma distinta, literalmente modificó mapas, añadio enemigos, y una historia mejor contada, sin mencionar el hecho de que la primera versión que jugué las mecanicas de movimiento eran muy reducidas, me refiero algo como "dar un ataque en el aire, o el tener la patada aerea" eran mejoras notables, ahora literalmente tienes amuletos que te dan hasta un triple salto, dashes ilimitados en el aire, mejores perks y mas arboles de habilidades, la historia literalmente tenia sentido, thalamus antes solo era un boss easter egg, si llevabas un amuleto oxidado, ahora no solo esta incluido en la historia sino que si completas 5 bosses en heroico (son inmortales, no mueren, pide revancha, pide la revancha te pierdes la mitad del juego si no lo haces) accedes de la nada a zonas ocultas e incluso 2 finales con thalamus, literalmente el combate final rompe la 4ta pared y me hizo acojonarme cuando leyó el nombre de usuario de mi pc, el juego es muy completo aunque la unica pega que le podria poner es: 1. por alguna razón no me reconoce hacer un logro muy basico 2. una de las habilidades mas destructivas del juego, de la guadaña para ser exactos, hay veces que pega y otras que le apetece ser pacifista y no hacer nada. 3.el prota tiene unos mommy issues que no puede con ello (como para no tenerlo)  te recomiendo mucho este juego, esta hecho con mucho amor y merece todo, peleas desafiante, momentos en los que lloras, risas y plot twist bastante xd (aun no puedo como es que en un souls 2D, hay una ciudad tecnologicamente superior llena de mechas asesinos)

This game was really good and a really fun metroidvania, albeit with some balancing issues with adds and such during boss fights, situations where you could be ping ponged between enemies and such, but that's all fine and acceptable. What isn't acceptable is to have the re-fights be excruciatingly hard by just making the boss tankier, hit for more, spam attacks, and throw out adds at a rate where you simply cannot avoid and/or block them all. I would honestly be fine with this, it's a good challenge for those that want it. BUT in order to obtain the actual ending of the game you have to defeat FIVE of them while keeping your deaths under a certain threshold or you are just locked out of a satisfying end to this game. Genuinely one of the most fundamentally flawed gimmicks I have ever seen in a game.

It is a very mediocre game. Moving through the levels feels super bland. The biomes are pretty short and meet the bosses fast. You have to move back and forth a lot but you have no fast travel in early and mid game.  There are a lot of mechanics in the game that make no sense. There is no shortcut button to the map. You can place markers on the map, but you cannot customize them. It is also really hard to aim where to put the markers with a gamepad. This whole thing feels like it was added as an afterthought?  Exploring isn't rewarding. You get consumbles, but you reach the carry cap very fast. Similar for the talent tree. There is nothing in there that really adds to the gameplay or makes you excited for progress. I tried playing as a spellcaster, but could rarely use my spells due to short windows. Spells two cast times and there is no time to use them during boss fights. Spells also don't do that much damage, you are usually better off just attacking. Attacks deal as much (probably more) damage and are less risky. Enemies variety and AI are absolute trash. Enemies just stand there inactively. As a ranged spellcaster, by the time they noticed and started walking towards me, they were almost dead. The hit boxes are also trash. At one point, I got bored with destroying everything on range and tried to play with the counter mechanic. With bosses I noticed alot that their attacks rarely hit you if you stand inside them. Like, neither a counter proc nor did I get damage. Wtf? The parry windows are also suuuuper huge, completly trivializing the game. Story and presentation of it are not interesting. There were a few nice music pieces, but most are unmemorable. Voice acting is nothing special.
