[h1]Death Gambit: Afterlife - этим пытают после смерти. [/h1]  Такое должно быть стыдно выпускать в ранний доступ, не то что на релиз. Игра полна багов, отсутствия баланса и здравого смысла при разработке.  [h2]Баги [/h2]  - не редко не срабатывают нужные скрипты в сюжетных местах, по итогу твой персонаж остается заперт между двумя стеклянными стенами. Помогает выйти, зайти и начать с контрольной точке.  - не редко пропадают перья. Да, я в курсе, что после смерти у тебя отнимают одно перо и надо вернуться к месту гибели и подобрать его. Я про то, что как раз пера может и не быть на месте гибели и трать свои честно заработанные души на возврат  - с какого момента перестала расти полоска здоровья после прокачки уровня (любого параметра, который добавляет хп). Цифры растут на бумаге, а не деле - нет. Есть аура, которая даёт доп хп, и она также не работает с этого момента.  - Нельзя поменять магию. Да и фиг с ней, подумал я, прошёл игру полностью без неё. Но есть нюансы, например, финального босса можно пройти только если повезёт, если он не насрет мобами в щитах (а щиты у таких мобов разбиваются как раз магией).  [h2]Баланс и маразм [/h2]  А точнее, их отсутствие и присутствие  Делим на 2 аспекта: а) оружие и снаряжение  У того и у другого есть прокачка. До +5 точется одним ресурсом, до +10 - другим. Первый ресурс падает крайне редко, но все таки к концу игры можно нафармить, чтобы все 4 вещи (оружие, плащ, шлем, сапоги) качнуть на +5. Но. По-первых, ты качаешь вещи только ближе к концу, когда понимаешь, какие вещи самые крутые и что есть смысл качать. Второе. Если ты качаешь какое-то барахло периодически, то тебе не хватит на хорошие вещи.  Второй ресурс. Во-первых, этот ресурс даёт тебе 1000 душ, что довольно много, но никто тебе не говорит, что этот ресурс нужен будет на прокачку от +5. Так что сливаешь этот ресурс сразу же, как находишь. Во-вторых, этого ресурса крайне мало на всю игру. В игре есть достижение "вкачать любой предмет до максимума" и можно найти шлем +9, который нужно лишь раз точнуть и будет ачивка. Так вот. После того, как я узнал про этот ресурс, я даже половину необходимого не смог найти. Я уже не говорю, чтобы честно вкачать предмет от +5 до 10, а то, что у тебе надо будет качать ещё 3 предмета, разработчик вообще не думает.  Полный бред. Типа, мы вам дали прокачку, но прокачать снаряжение вы не сможете, потому что у вас ресурсов нет.  [h2]Боссы и начало духоты[/h2] К боссам претензии нет, пока а) не началась снежная локация; б) пока не ковыряешь боссов в легендарном режиме.  Не смотря на все баги и недоработки, игра для меня окончательно умерла именно с началом снежной локации. Здесь полностью из игры выкачали кислород и началась полная духота.  И тут мы снова говорим про баланс.  У мобов и боссов резко возрастает количество хп, и ковырять приходиться их часами, при этом ты никак не можешь повысить свой урон. Условно, у тебя урона 200, но чтобы было комфортно убивать босса (так же как и предыдущих), нужно хотя бы 500 дамага. И тут во-первых, пушку не вкачать по причиннам озвученных выше (да и она не поможет, каждый +1 даёт примерно 3 дамага), во-вторых, и уровень ты не можешь качнуть сильно. К концу игры у меня был примерно 110ур, а каждый +1 к параметру сила, даст тебе примерно +1 к урону. Т е тебе нужно качнуть уровней 300, чтобы поправить баланс, что нереально.  [h2]Легендарная духота[/h2]  Бить боссов в легендарном режиме особый сорт извращенства. Некоторые боссы, это наглухо сломанные и несбалансированные животные. И тут хочу их поделить на 3 типа:  а) адекватные, их всего несколько. У которых чуть больше хп, чуть выше урон, чуть больший мувсет, чем у оригинала  б) абсолютно сломанные. Здесь не только миллиард хп у босса, но и кнопку сама атаками не отжимает вообще. Яркий пример такого босса - Forgotten Gaian. У этого ублюдка не просто хп дохера, так и призывает он бомжей на помощь настолько часто, что не успеваешь убить первую партию, как лезет вторая. А когда босса бить?  в) наполовину сломанные. У боссов адекватное количество хп, но они бесконечно могут спамить атаками. Так что убить их можно только если повезёт. Яркий пример - Tundra Lord. В первой фазе этого боя он может чисто тебя заспамить атаками, что ни увернуться, ни отхилиться возможности нет.  [h2]Сюжет [/h2]  Или как без сдачи крови определить, что человек десятки лет употребляет тяжелые наркотики.  Вот есть сборная солянка, где куча всего, но сбалансировано и вкусно, здесь же нахватали куски сюжета с разных мест и получилась окрошка на квасе. Согласен, найдутся любители и на такое, но и дристанет вас потом знатно.  [h2]Новая игра+ или окончательное удушение духотой [/h2]  Вы часто видели игры, в которых после нажатия кнопки "Новая игра+" у вас начинается новая игра +? Ну наверно в 99% случаев.  Но эти гении попадают именно в тот самый один процент. После "начала новый игры+" на игрока тоннами валится бессмысленный и не нужный сюжет, затем финальный босс, затем опять тонна говна, затем финальный босс финального босса, затем ещё куча ходьбы, затем ну окончательный финальный финал после финального босса. Я такого порочного круга финальных финалов даже в Атаке Титанов не помню.  В общем, после финального босса (кстати, ещё один баг, на второй стадии он перестал мне дамаг наносить, но хп у него тонна осталась, так что вариант того, что так и задумано и это фиктивный бой, не рассматриваю), на тебя опять выливают тучу сюжетного [strike]говна[/strike] мастерства, но там дали ачивку за прохождение, я сгорел окончательно, нажал alt f4 и больше туда не заходил.  [h2]Последние капли духоты [/h2]  - Так же в игре до финального босса нет быстрых перемещений между кострами. Это огромный минус для любой метроидвании, а здесь еще и под конец дают, как будто издевается разработчик. Только нахер они уже нужны, если способности, чтобы дособирать весь лут, дают гораздо раньше? Насколько вообще должно быть высокое ЧСВ и разрабов, чтобы заставлять игроков 100 раз бегать по своим локациям, думают, это настолько интересно?  Какой вывод можно сделать из всего этого. Однозначно, бесповоротно и неоспоримо, передо мной, на моих глазах, с большим отрывом от второго места Ghost Song (тоже редкостная помойка), оказалась ХУДШАЯ метроидвания, в которую я когда-либо играл (а так как это мой любимый жанр, поиграл я во всякое)  Но есть нюанс, это все ещё не худшая игра, так как есть ещё игры от UBISOFT

Good game, good soulsvania, very souls-y, it expects you to have multiple playthroughs and that's a bit annoying for someone that doesn't really enjoy NG+ (or at least not more than once). Exploration is fine, boss battles are enjoying and challenging enough for when you're supposed to do them. Heroics are generally a good challenge and I think balanced ok, with some exceptions (the most busted one being the Eldrich dude like wtf why is that fight harder than the end game bosses). Builds are ok tho equipment (armor) is a bit simplistic. Pacing felt a bit weird at times, a lot of back and forth and at some points I felt more lost than I think I should've (not talking about the zones that are especially designed to be confusing/secret-y).  A bit burnt out now but I'll probably do a NG+ for Ending B as I wanna experience that last boss fight.  And yes I do recommend this to any metroidvania enjoyer, but be aware, if you're a completionist, some achievments are very difficult.  The story felt very confusing for like half of the game, maybe more. That's also my fault because I haven't watched the intro the game has, that kinda gives you a start that I missed initially. Towards the end, the story becomes more interesting and it, alongside a specific boss battle, have some very intersting ideas (especially if played on PC).  Oh and very sad that I couldn't play this game in borderless full screen. Whatever I tried, it was either FS or Border so I played it maximised on window because I hate not being able to have the second monitor usable with the game non-minimized.

值得尝试的冷门良作，游戏中的角色打破第四面墙与观众互动的形式较为新颖，玩家作为“观测者”一举一动都能直接影响剧情发展，隐藏boss甚至要求玩家通过改变现实从而影响游戏内容。  不过丰富的创作欲使得作者将自己的想法一股脑全塞进了游戏流程，导致游戏后期在杂糅了各种稀奇古怪的想法后略显混乱。  剧情见仁见智，虽然情节设计老套，但是呈现效果良好，人物刻画立体饱满。最大短板就是怪物受击反馈差导致毫无打击感，什么类型武器普攻都是轻飘飘的，还好弹反手感不错。  build玩法丰富，没有任何代价的洗点让玩家在同一周目能体验各种流派的玩法（死法）。  在地图探索方面做了减法，一到新区域就会给整张区域地图，这样即使地图设计一般，对玩家的引导倒是没问题了。音乐丰富优秀，美术设计优秀。

The combat and movement mechanics were fine. The other game-play systems felt stapled together but seemed... fine? My main gripe with the game-play was the uninteresting skill trees: I never felt excited when I received a skill point. I thought the concept of the "WILL" stat was intriguing (it's a counter that goes up when you die and is reduced when you defeat boss rematches) and it tied into the story well, but it also felt punishing in the context of some of the true ending requirements.  The story/plot/tone (especially that of the true ending [spoiler] I mean good lord, fighting the final boss three times? Seriously?[/spoiler]) was all over the place and sometimes emotionally condescending to the player. Tonally, it wanted to be both Dark Souls and Undertale, but it didn't strike any sort of balance. It acts like you've become super attached to the characters despite only having interactions where you interact to exhaust dialog and then move on. The twist was also pretty predictable, but a twist being predictable isn't necessarily a bad thing.   While the style and aesthetic isn't particularly distinctive, it does look great. The full voice-over was a welcome surprise ([spoiler]one of the few story bits that got me was when Soren finally talked![/spoiler]) and the OST is lovely. The character, boss, and enemy designs were fun.   If you don't care about story it's a fine game. Plays fine, looks great and was fun to explore. If you [i]do[/i] care about story then get it on sale. Plenty of people have loved this game and despite my gripes I did enjoy it enough to finish it.

death's gambit is a huge game that is full of ideas but almost none of the ideas are its own.  it's got soulslike leveling but with almost no reason to invest in stats besides health and one damage stat of your choosing. it's got more or less the most standard suite of modern troidlike movement upgrades, with one sort of interesting exception. it's got skill trees and unique equipment options and abilities for days; it feels almost like an arpg, but the bosses are uninteractive and prone to quickly putting huge hitboxes on the screen, so the best strategy almost always seems to be to just block/dodge and punish with something quick regardless of what kind of build you're trying to run. it's got a story that sometimes feels like it's trying to say something but then forgets what it's saying mid-thought and then tries to say it again later but forgets again. the tone flip-flops between quirky and dead serious (lol) but not in a way that feels clever or meaningful, it just kind of does it. the main antagonist and most important moments involving him are ripped straight from undertale.  the current top review already mentioned this but this game feels like a mess of ideas from games the devs liked but it doesn't manage to come together into anything more than the sum of its parts. i don't think every game needs to try to reinvent the wheel but it should at least try to make the wheel its own and death's gambit doesn't do that. if you want a soulslike, troidlike, or even a soulslike troidlike, you'd probably be better served elsewhere.  all that said...the game did manage to do something cool just often enough to keep me interested all the way to the end, even if that something cool was usually derivative of other works. if you got this game in a bundle, i'd say it's at least worth a shot; some people seem to really like this game and you might be one of them.

There is nothing more important when making a souls-like than balance and unfortunately the balance in this game is beyond frustrating. It starts off fine, decently challenging and not too difficult but past the halfway point the game loses all fun and just becomes a chore. Bosses take 800 hits to kill, and the times where you aren't swarmed with 5+ enemies at once you encounter an enemy that can one shot you even when you are high level and have upgraded equipment. I have done everything I can to make it more manageable, such as grinding and exploring every nook and cranny but I just can't do it anymore.   Side note: while there are ways to manipulate it, having the true ending directly tied to how many times you die in a game that's suppose to be difficult is needlessly frustrating and makes every mistake feel even that more punishing.

Death's Gambit: Afterlife is a beefed up version of a soulsvania that was released a while ago. It took me a few hours to really warm up to it, but once I did, I found a rewarding, combat-focused game that finds some clever design choices that side-step some of the more irritating aspects of soulsborne-likes.   In this dark fantasy world, there is a decaying kingdom where an object that can grant immortality lies. Many have ventured to the ruins to claim such a coveted treasure, but none have made it past the immortal protectors who guard the relic. Sorun, the player character, is the latest soldier to head for Caer Soirse at the behest of his king, and he and his fellow compatriots scarcely step foot within the city outskirts before they are decimated by an immortal warrior.   That should be the end of Sorun, but it isn't. Death has a vested interest in claiming the relic so that he is not put out of business, so he grants Sorun an undying body in exchange for destroying the source of immortality. Sorun agrees as he has another motive for entering the city, as he is searching for his mother who went missing on a prior expedition.   As the synopsis suggests, Death's Gambit: Afterlife has a more traditional structure than the usual soulsborne method of learning what happened to a ruined kingdom by looking at depressing locales and reading vague, disjointed bits of lore in item descriptions. There are actual characters with clearly defined motives and personalities and this game has a sense of humor. A very ironic, dark sense of humor. A special note has to be made of Death Himself, a witty, sarcastic little stinker that often breaks the fourth wall with his self-aware remarks. He might be my favorite personification of death ever.  As I mentioned earlier, it took me about two hours to really get the hang of the controls. They give you a lot of skills and play-style options right from the beginning and the button the layout feels a little awkward because several moves (like the ground slide that breaks enemy shield defenses) is a two button combo. But I adapted with some practice.   As I mentioned, the standard soulsvania stuff is all here and accounted for: slow-paced and methodical combat that punishes mistakes, ability-gated progression that rewards exploration and backtracking to earlier zones, tough boss fights, and corpse-running, albeit a more forgiving version than we are used to seeing.   One of the key features that makes this game a bit for accessible is that when you die, you don't lose your souls. Instead you drop a healing feather, this game's version of an estus flask. You can either do the corpse run or pay some souls to have it retrieved. Stuck on a boss? Upon death, you are rewarded with some souls based on how much of the health bar you whittled down so you can increase you stats by a level or two before trying again.   Exploration also makes the bosses a bit easier because you can find tomes scattered around the world that will give you a damage boost against the bosses. There are two per boss for a max of a 10% increase. This is not to say the game's a cake walk. It might make the earlier game a breeze for seasoned soulsborne players who are used to the genre, but these training wheels might make this a easier game for newcomers to get into.   The difficulty really ramps up in the late game. I got Ending C in my playthrough, the normal end. There is a significant bit of content that comes after that point, but it's skill-gated behind some tough optional bosses. I would probably need to play the game a least one more time with a different build to feel brave enough to take on these behemoths as I found the final bosses for ending C about at my current skill ceiling.   I, unfortunately, chose the blood-knight as my starting class, a strength based build that specializes in going full throttle with the axe. I only found two axes in my play-through, the second one at the beginning of the final area. I checked the wiki. There are three axes total. It was able to change my weapon to a greatsword when the starter axe just could not keep up with enemies anymore, but most of my passive abilities from the skill tree were based around the axe. It sucks that this one class is useless at endgame.   The bosses are phenomenal. Some of the best mechanics-wise I've experienced in this genre.   Fast travel comes into play ridiculously late. Honestly, you should have gotten it around the midway point as you'll be criss-crossing the map in search the magical McGuffins to open the way to immortal city, but you don't get it until you are practically at the end of the game.   I really enjoyed Death's Gambit: Afterlife. But for the praise I have for it, I can't help for think that if I was in the mood for a dark soulsvania game, I would pick up Blasphemous and the Last Faith before replaying this one. It's good. Great even in parts, but there few bad things I have to say about it really did have a big impact on my final thoughts about it. It measured up just an inch too short to stand shoulder to shoulder with the titans of the genre.

思来想去给这游戏差评，感觉它是金玉其外，败絮其内。 刚一上手感觉这游戏素质特别高，内容乍一看很丰富，但实际体验下来简直灾难。 我玩的法师，放个法术还要召唤元素球，总共就3个球。强度堪比塔的机宝。前中期boss一般的时候还能放放法术，等到后期英雄难度的时候，根本没机会放法术，dps太低了，甚至不如普攻来的快。 装备看似丰富，结果就几个模板的换皮，我玩法师，火焰魔导书从头用到尾，本来以为是类魂应该装备丰富，没升装备，结果加智力的装备寥寥无几，基本也算是从头用到尾。莫名其妙的物品数量上限，就不能学学魂放个收藏箱吗？ 虽然也是学魂，职业和装备属性无强制关系，但是天赋和职业相关，等我发现法师好菜的时候已经无法挽回了，天赋全是法师天赋，根本转不动。 地图设计一坨，无意义的地图连接，初见新图发现有很多岔路，但是每个都走一遍后发现，尼玛好多都是锁死的，门不能从这面打开，结果还是只有一条路可走，还是一本道的设计。而且让初级地图和高级地图连接的意义是什么，基本就不会再去走那条路，尤其是后期有传送的时候。说到传送，给的时候太晚了，游戏大部分时候都要在中间骑马，传送一般都是游戏中期解锁，但是这游戏后期才解锁，解锁后下一个主线就是选择结局分支，实在是太晚了。 小地图偷工减料，后面一整块区域就一个方块就打发完了，一点都不上心。 英雄boss就是一坨，没玩的时候看评论对伤害检测不以为然，然后被血虐后深刻意识到，英雄boss那技能就几何不可能全躲了，实际就要爆输出速杀boss避免拖入僵局。 而且还学了魂的死npc设计，开局商人对话后不买东西就会敌对，老家的npc也会因为xxx情况死亡，太尼玛离谱了，天天学黑魂不按预定选项就会死npc的脑瘫设计，这设计到法环都基本没有了。 残酷走廊就不像是正常人设计的，超长的跳跳乐还充满了堆怪和无存档，跑过的方法还是用无敌无限跳这种方式逃课。因为血量有限还要一次一次从头打。人家痛苦之路再难，也有回血的build，至少不用在意跑酷中死亡。 最后结局感觉作者在放飞自我，想搞一个众人灵魂力量击败boss，演出却很糟糕。机械未来科技等元素根本没有做到和背景融合，就像凭空出现一样。meta元素太过突兀，不会作者感觉搞个绿色的代码指令做过场动画很帅吧。 还有一点可能是我自己电脑的问题，地图切换加载太长了，升级加个点都会黑屏。游戏速度也是时快时慢，尤其是每次暂停、

Short answer: Get it on sale. High-quality picturebook, mid(maybe low)-quality game. If you like the "adventure" bit of action/adventure then buy,  Long answer: Amazing characters, great art, solid story, great voice acting, good humour. Downside is movement and combat don't feel good. Invincibility frames are awkwardly placed, damage seems to be random from area-to-area. A few jumping "puzzles" also really toe the line between exploit/shoddy coding and skill application. Lack of boss music in a lot of places as well- not sure if that was intentiona, or if budget/timeline didn't allow for it. Either way, some of the fights kind of suck for it, since they lack interesting mechanics or intensity to make up for the boilerplate exploration track that plays.   This game's trying to be too much at once without the scaffolding for it, and it buckles under its own weight sometimes.   All that said. I still enjoy. Glad I bought

It's good so far. Combat is great, but the focus on trying to make a Dark Souls metroidvania feels particularly unoriginal with this title. With a game like Blasphemous, the developers really put their own spin on it, whereas with Death's Gambit it feels like too many things are exact replications of Dark Souls features. This isn't necessarily terrible, but I do wish that Death's Gambit relied on more of the developer's own original ideas or at least disguised it better. It's okay to borrow things from other games, but it should never be that obvious. The occasional homage or easter egg is fine, but when multiple mechanics and design aspects are blatantly taken and just given a new coat of paint, this is when it becomes an issue of originality. That doesn't mean that there wasn't a lot of work that went into this game; it's a good game and a good addition to the genre, but I hope in the future these developers will try harder to make something original.     Occasional bugs, but nothing major or game breaking.

タイトルに「Afterlife 」がつく前に一度クリアしていたが改めてプレイ。 戦闘面は楽しく、タイミングよくガードすることで大ダメージの反撃ができ、ガードがダメージ100%カットのため攻撃よりも防御を重視した戦い方が求められる。 倒したボスの高難易度版との再戦ができることも高評価 また、日本語訳についても機械翻訳的なところが多くみられるがプレイに支障がないレベルにはなっている。 （回避攻撃が回転攻撃になっているのが少し困惑する位。おそらくロールが回避ではなく回転で翻訳されていそう）  ただ戦闘面以外が非常に残念な出来でプレイ中何度もイライラさせられ、これを理由に低評価にしています。 いくらでも悪い点はかけてしまうので、大きな問題を3点ほど以下に記載します。  [b] ・インベントリの使いづらさ [/b] 新しいアイテムを入手したときにアイテム欄の一番後ろに追加され、一番後ろまで行くには複数回のページ送りが必要になる。 インベントリのサイズが固定のため逆にページ送りしても空のページが表示されるため、手軽に拾ったアイテムを選択する方法がない。 また装備の仕方も面倒で、一般的なものだと装備で武器を選択すると所持している武器一覧が表示されて選択することになると思うが、このゲームの場合装備欄は装備を外すことしかできないため膨大なアイテム一覧から目的の装備を探す必要になる。  また、コントローラーのメニューとかを開くのに使うボタンが二つあると思うのだが割り当てが異常に偏っている。 以下のようになっているのだがポーズに割り当てるくらいならマップを割り当ててほしい。 またアビリティは休憩ポイントでしか変更できないので、メニューから確認できるメリットはほとんどない。  ボタン1：ポーズ ボタン2：アイテム・アビリティ・タレント・マップ・書物・設定など（ZR・ZLボタンで切り替え）  [b] ・武器バランスの悪さ [/b] このゲームではSTR・FIN・INTのどれか1パターンの育成をすることになるのだが、それぞれのステータスで使用できる武器が決まる。 STRは直剣・大剣・斧槍・盾、FINは短剣・槍・鎌・弓、INTが魔導書。 ただ最初に書いた通りこのゲームはガードが強いのでSTR1択といっても過言ではない。 また、自身の攻撃で相手の飛び道具を打ち消せるため、攻撃範囲の広い剣系統が強く短剣はほかの武器と組み合わせないとしんどい。  [b] ・初期クラスの選択[/b] 最初に選んだクラスは変更できないため重要な選択になるのだが、なぜかクラスのスキル（タレント）の確認ができず武器のモーションの確認しかできない。 最初に持っていけるアイテムも同様でアイテムの説明とか見ることができない。

一款稍显冷门的银河恶魔城良作！这个游戏非常的具有特色，在我看来应该是和渎神一样火的 欲扬先抑先说说我觉得很有问题的地方 -打击感真的太差了！明显能感觉到制作组在很多地方是很用心的，为什么打击感感觉就跟没有做一样？？？我的镰刀就像纸糊的一样砍来砍去。弹反也是没有任何打击感，只听见duang的一声然后舒爽的来个上勾拳，有些滑稽 -背包有物品储存上限，我还不知道上限是多少，不理解这样设计是意义何在，每个装备想存一件中后期都要爆仓的，真的很莫名其妙的设计 其他让我硬挑缺点的话我还真挑不出来了 ------------------------------------------------ 然后是个人偏爱的一些地方：游戏的音乐我很喜欢，地图的设计很有想法，以一只马的行进路线贯穿地图的中间线，后期开启传送。我经常在游戏开始的时候想过我不玩了我要回家，我要往回走，大部分游戏不是空气墙就是根本无路可退，这个游戏你是真的可以回家放弃，还专门设立了这样一个结局我喜欢。游戏的剧情十分的放飞自我，感觉编剧应该看过迪迦，最后吸收大家的光来战胜黑暗确实绷不住了。我很喜欢剧情能解释游戏开头和周目的设计，游戏的结尾死神对电脑前的你对话也令人会心一笑。 ------------------------------------------------ 接下来讲讲游戏关键的一环：boss与战斗 战斗上没有什么很特殊的内容在，依然是平砍翻滚弹反战技组合。 有一些boss是必须靠弹反才好打的，按键为举盾攻击，流程中的boss最多10次内就能过了，而流程boss打死后你可以回到boss场地打英雄形态，可以多次刷，奖励会一直掉。 但是！英雄boss难度大多数个人体感是翻三倍，这个时候！！！你就必须接触到本游戏战斗内容的一个特色（但大部分人不喜欢，我也不喜欢），就是输出检测机制，你必须要动脑子在有限的时间如何利用手法打出更高的伤害，这个时候我才明白天赋树的一些增伤技能或被动的设计，以主副武器和战技的联动来考虑自己的输出轴，举个例子，我个人是匕首打完五连获取副武器增伤换镰刀收割技能打伤害，接着换匕首五连斩然后镰刀收割打三轮打到镰刀爆攻速再用镰刀来输出，打那几个英雄修脚boss不得不这样。很多英雄boss你在短时间内不能造成充足的伤害来消除造物就会导致后面很不利，特别是大星术士英雄难度我打了100次，那个机制我一度以为我在打单人raid本。大星老师的英雄难度通关会给你当前手里的近战武器+9，有能力的可以刷他的英雄难度给自己手里的武器都升到+9。

Game is impressively simple and fun. I like Metroidvanias, top 3 genres, for sure. The end game is a little overly zealous with the heroic bosses being ridiculously hard and my processor can't keep up with Amulvaro AT ALL but I really enjoyed this game. I will play it again, in the future. It's more forgiving than other 'souls-like' games in the sense that you don't lose your currency when you die, just drop one of your healing items you have to go reclaim, but that's alright. Not everything has to be an immense struggle; that's what reality is for.   The plot is okay, I guess. There's nothing really surprising about it, I put everything together..pretty quick. The 'big twist' at the end isn't really much of a twist, just more of a "Protagonist is dumb as rocks" moment. After the first couple of scenes with Endless, we kinda all knew where things were going except for Soren. [spoiler] Although, I must say, I genuinely did not see the "Go fight these robots in an underground factory that are commanded by a lightning lizard" part coming. I guess that was..kinda neat..? Bysurge is alright, just stupidly easy on normal and then viciously difficult for all the wrong reasons on heroic. [/spoiler]  My only complaint is sometimes I wish Death would shut up and just let me play the game. I died to a flower and he made fun of me for it. Not cool, man..

Все что нужно для хорошей метроидвании. Интересные боссы, разнообразные красивейшие локации, трогательный сюжет и куча отсылок и секретов (апогей достигается тогда, когда узнаешь, что какую-то информацию для полнейшего прохождения нужно брать из внеигрового контента).  Сюжет. Кажется непонятным и поверхностным, лора здесь практически нет. Когда попадаешь в Гарде Тум, то вообще возникает вопрос, откуда в таком сеттинге какие-то фантастические элементы? Но ближе к концу все встает на свои места, эпичность последнего часа игры максимальная (я не про концовку С).  Кстати, не обязательно перепроходить игру ради концовки А. Слышала, что люди жалуются на какие-то небывалые сложности, чтобы ее открыть, что нужно убить 5 героических боссов, еще и не умирать. На самом деле все далеко не так страшно. К концу первого прохождения уровень можно докачать до 150 (в игре есть пара мест для удобного фарма эссенсии + ауры на получение большего ее количества). На таком уровне первые 5 боссов в героическом режиме будут намного проще, чем даже при первом их убийстве. Кроме того каждый такой босс будет убавлять 40 воли, а в итоге она должна быть меньше 100. То есть за все прохождение можно спокойно умереть 199 раз и все равно получить концовку А. Если же одолеете 7 героических, то можно умереть целых 279 раз, это еще постараться надо!  Геймплей. Играла за большой меч и за кинжалы+копье и оба прохождения получились достаточно разнообразными по геймплею. За персонажа силы в бою больше отталкиваешься от щита, есть много вариантов оружия, которое может облегчить прохождение (с уроном от тьмы или электричества). Единственное, что на геймпаде неудобно - парирование, но можно обойтись и идеальным блоком. За персонажа ловкости  пользуешься преимущественно перекатами, скорость отклика персонажа другая - это хорошо ощущается, соответственно тактика боя тоже иная. Таланты, способности и предметы - все было непохожим. За мага геймплей скорее всего тоже будет сильно отличаться, поэтому игру пройти будет не скучно как минимум 3 раза.  Говорят, что не очень удобная карта с малым количеством меток. Мне было ее вполне достаточно, боссы и идолы показаны, локации подписаны, как будто остальное уже слишком бы упрощало прохождение. В некоторых играх жанра карты вообще нет. Если привык после каждого поворота сверяться с картой, то наверное будет не супер удобно. Хотя в большинстве локаций карта довольно подробная и по ней можно обнаружить скрытые комнаты и проходы. Ближе к концу игры появится ТП по идолам, поэтому можно будет быстро найти все оставшиеся секреты и перемещаться по героическим боссам.  Атмосфера. Она здесь есть и погружает в себя медленно, но верно. Помогает красивый пиксель-арт, музыка, озвучка персонажей и внимание к детялям. Отдельно хочется упомянуть, как хорошо второстепенные персонажи своей нелепостью и юмором разбавляют временами темную атмосферу игры. Один только ресторанчик чего стоит. Ну и конечно музыка. Тема Темного рыцаря в героическом режиме просто <3 В итоге 9/10. Есть некоторые косяки, но общие впечатления очень хорошие, хочется в нее возвращаться. Всем морковок!

I feel like this game is underrated. IMO, it blends together souls-like and metroidvania perfectly and the presentation (visual and audio) is superb. I can't even tell that it's an indie title. The voice acting is also very good. The thing I liked the most was that there are so many different play-styles/build for you to choose from because you pick a main class and can then choose a second class later (you only get the class tree for that second class, not the inherent benefits or general talents) and there are many talents, abilities, and weapon types for you to mix and match to create a build. Also, there are many ways of evading/countering depending on your class choices like dodging, parrying, deflecting (perfectly timed blocking + special guard counter right after), or just straight up taking hits and retaliating right after (Bloodborne-style). The boss fights were fun but not too challenging, but perhaps my build was just strong - I did notice that there isn't much usage of the metroidvania/traversal skills during boss fights though (unlike Aeterna Noctis or Grime). I do VERY much appreciate that all of the mentioned ways of evading/countering are perfectly usable during most boss fights (unlike Dark Souls 3 or Elden Ring).

It's a decent souls-like metroidvania with a few mistakes. The game doesn't explain a lot with items and systems etc so you'll want a walkthrough. Also I used a longsword and used 2 total weapons in like 15-20 hours. Also with other equipment it doesn't show up on your character and basically is just small stat upgrades so pretty boring. The skill tree is mostly boring with stat upgrades and small changes like "pressing interact button for 15% healing" which you'll never do. None of the skills I gained changed my playstyle or was anything interesting. Similar to weapons, I got 3 total abilities for my weapon all game and that happened within the first 5 hours, so same repetition.   The game commits the worst and dumbest decision of making fast travel unavailable until 5% left in the game, which makes no sense, because you basically won't use it at all but don't have it when you need it and I didn't use it once. To not have a proper fast travel in a metroidvania is a brainless decision and loses major points. The story is meh and I skipped most if it. But the game is decent length, with a lot of systems and depth as well as tons of bosses. It checks most of the boxes. 7.5/10.

Vamos lá.....  Death's Gambit é um MetroidVania com pegada soulslike que tinha tudo pra ser bem ambicioso. Porém não é um jogo pra qualquer um. Se você vier pensando que será um jogo fácil não é pra você. O sistema de drops eu particularmente não achei muito bom, principalmente se você iniciar um NG+ e ainda for lutar com os chefes no modo heroico . E aqui é um dos problemas que eu particularmente achei bem irritante. O combate com os chefes nesse modo, em alguns casos, é muito, mas muito desbalanceado, chega a ser ridículo o dano que você leva de um chefe que ta no nível 100 e você no nível 150 por exemplo.  A história é um pouco confusa e não te instiga a se aprofundar nela, assim o enredo é meio fraco.Como eu não solto spoiler e nem gosto de por resumos dos jogos em análises, jogue e tire suas próprias conclusões ok?  No resto é um bom jogo sim e acabo recomendando caso esses dois estilos de jogo sejam seus favoritos (soulslike e Metroidvania).  Notas:  Jogabilidade: 7/10 História: 5/10 Dificuldade: 8/10 (desbalanceada em muitos momentos) Gráficos: 7/10 (bonitinho até) Trilha sonora: 6/10 Público: Nichado eu diria  Recomendo que compre em uma boa promoção, experimente, e tire suas próprias conclusões.

Pretty old game but I'm completely satisfied that I eventually picked it up. I know that his game had a rough start but I think with its current build it is a satisfying Metroidvania with Souls-Like elements.   There is enough variety to in terms of weapons and combat styles to go around in, and I believe players have the means to experiment if they invest enough time farming the necessary currency to change builds mid-game.  The game is quite lengthy as well if you plan on completing everything there is to the game, with two possible endings that can be unlocked through a new game+ feature. The game features "Heroic" boss rematches to shower you with frustration, and completing every single encounter is tied to unlocking the two endings of the game. There is a speed run challenge as well, which unfortunately is something I won't be personally gunning for. For masochists out there, this feature might be for you.   I think the best feature of the game for me is that it has made me a believer of timing parries and blocks. No other game has forced my hand to learn this feature yet as most games, even actual Souls titles let you go through the game without the need for it. I hope this new appreciation of the skill carries over to other titles as it was very rewarding to learn and do so in this game.   Worth the pick-up to scratch your Metroidvania and Souls-Like itch.

A delightful Metroidvanai full of great exploration. The story has depth and meaning to it providing a great motivation for the player. Overall casually playing through and completing the game will feel satisfying and memorable.  That said the Death's Gambit sorely lacks in a few areas as the player progresses towards end-game and DLC content. A completionist will suffer as there is no true "completing" due to the divided paths Afterlife offers leaving map and item completion as an impossibility. Moreover a person who avoids spoilers and online guides will find themselves wandering aimlessly for hours as the game actively breaks the 4th wall and *requires* an individual to use online resources to complete certain end-game actions while withholding such information. Hints are dropped that the world outside the game is in play but not where or why. The developers cleverly used community engagement to progress the game's story and perhaps if you were involved in such community activities in 2021 this disparity would be different; however, if you are reading this review today then you, as well as I, were a few years too late for that information.  For someone to truly enjoy this game they must let go of the idea of 100% and online spoilers. If you can do that you will find the game to be full of twists and unique concepts other Metroidvanias lack; however, if you enjoy a blind play-through be prepared to be stuck and frustrated as you navigate the perils of both the game and spoilers for yourself.

Let's update this now that I forced myself to finish what I paid for.  Death's Gambit is not shy about being a 2D Soulslike amalgamation of various games, struggling to find its own identity. No idea who nailed the Metroidvania tag on this game though.  Anyway... With one exception, Perfect Block is key. Not the most generous window, but I've seen far worse. Problem is, especially bosses can move through your character mid-animation and hit you in the back when attacking. But dying is par for the course with Soulslikes anyway.  What is not, is how much time dying wastes! Your character sinks to the floor, the screen fades to black, Death gracefully descends from above to reap your soul and ascend again. THEN it finally switches to a loading screen. Now you could hold a button to skip the whole animation, but for some reason you have to hold it for as long as the animation plays anyway... And if you do a spoiler thing, your character holds his head in pain for a moment after respawning before you can finally throw yourself into the meat grinder. Every. Single. Time.  Add to this a bug that decreases weapon attack if you upgrade a weapon and I can't recommend Death's Gambit.

This game slaps. It has no business being this good and having such great production value. Every area is carefully crafted, fully voice acted and it has really diverse gameplay. Also, the story is pretty solid and while it can take itself seriously at times,  it can also get very campy without being annoying.  Only gripe I would have is that some of the skills and abilities are on the math-side of 20% X this and 30% Y that, which isn't a really rewarding skill progression. On the flipside, you do get new weapons and items all the time, the game grows in difficulty and gets more dynamic, so overall not a dealbreaker or anything.  I really wonder why this game isn't *more* popular when compared to other similar bangers like Dead Cells, Axiom Verge or Salt and Sanctuary. And about that last one, if you liked S&S you will definitely LOVE Death's Gambit. I know the term is a bit worn out, but Death's Gambit really feels like a hidden gem.  Give it a chance.

Disclaimer: Despite the fact that my stats say I played this for only a couple of hours, I played it on my Steam Deck offline and that time is for some reason not reflected, which seems to be an issue with any game in general.  A pretty solid Metroidvania with a dash of Dark Souls thrown in. The art style is cool and it has an intriguing story and a ton of upgrades and items. I started playing it and fell into one of those "Just a bit more, until the next save point" states of mind and found myself playing it long enough to beat two different bosses and unlock a few upgrades. The only reason I didn't continue playing for a longer session was that I got to one of those points where you need to find where to go next on the map and I had some chores to do, so I stopped playing. At times it reminded me of the SNES Metroid and of Castlevania: SotN, both of which are good things. Between games like this one, the Blasphemous series and the Last Faith, I'm glad we have plenty of options even if major studios no longer care about these genres.  My only complaint is that it doesn't load if you're not online, which is IMO a stupid requirement for any game that is not by definition online co-op. You can go offline as soon as it boots, though, which is miles better than what happens with some games that simply won't work offline.  Edit: I forgot to include that the game has some unexpected humor in your interactions with Death every time you die, both because Death is kind of snarky and sarcastic and talks trash about you dying so often, and also because you sometimes run into him in surprising places doing equally surprising stuff.

You'll like this if 2D Dark Souls interests you. I think I'm getting a bit tired of Souls-likes but I still managed to finish this.  I wish the platforming was more fun but the exploration at least feels rewarding with the tomes.

EDIT: I've had a good go at new game plus and played with a couple of other classes now. The game is still definitely worth picking up, the story and first play through alone are worth it. But the flaws are beginning to come to a head in the NG+. The heroic bosses are starting to hit hard enough that issues that were easily ignored prior are becoming more apparent. In particular there are some issues with animations and hit boxes, not small things like things clipping each other but not counting as a hit either. There is an important skill for crowd control and chunking, the arrow of light skill. It's damn near necessary for the Cusithe boss fight which is why you will start noticing that it sometimes doesn't hit things. It was less notable in normal gameplay as the environment is diverse and not always flat, so you might dismiss it as being obstructed on terrain. The Cusithe boss arena is literally just a flat floor. The massive beam of light that you NEED to hit the lightning orbs Cusithe launches will randomly miss enemies in this scenario. It turns an already flawed boss fight into purely rng. It wouldn't be so frustrating but Cusithe is basically a copy of an earlier boss fight, the Ancient Gaian, which doesn't suffer this issue. It's not the only issue either, the over eager way landing a jump is implemented means your character will ignore any remaining air jumps you have (potentially unlimited depending on what upgrades you have) as it desperately tries to plant you on the ground. This makes a lot of timed puzzles with platforming significantly harder then they need to be as well as making mobile combat (in a game all about mobile combat) needlessly tedious and unpleasant. A good example is Cusithe again (you need to avoid horizontal beam attacks near the ground, how fun that is as you watch the remaining two air jumps you have become non-canon as the floor turns into giant magnet) but the worst offender is Amulvaro. His entire arena is made of floating platforms you can pass through and it would be one of the best fights in the game hands down if this "sticky" floor issue didn't make navigating his arena nearly impossible (once again adding unnecessary rng to a game that should be defined by you skill and playing within the confines of the system).  It's really shocking how much variation between boss fights there are. The grey wander, Ancient gaian, Bysurge, Dark knight and Eldritch council are amazing, great fun and some of the best 2D fights I've ever seen in a game. But then you get Cusithe, Kaern, Amulvaro and the Endless (specifically the lightning form) that either don't work well with the games system (or in Cusithe's case are just plain bad) and essentially become DPS checks (something that the game otherwise seems to push away from, you can sequence break if you can beat some bosses early, in particular heroic Bulwark lets you jump past phoenix and wraith king early, I love it when games reward you for doing things like that). Let me make the point, the good fights are absolutely worth powering through the bad ones. Heroic council is insane and exceptionally fun once you find a rhythm you're comfortable with, you even get to pick which order the battles advances in with the trade off being you're on a timer and the main boss accumulates phases before you go one on one with a combined uber phase.    It's like someone took a bunch of Terry Pratchett books and made a game out of them, but rather than a funny, if cynical point and click game it's a brutal, if unpolished, metroidvania. The world building and exploration are off the charts and the world is beautiful. You will have a lot of fun simply exploring the world and finding secrets. The combat is pretty good, some of the boss fights are spectacular, some of the best in the genre. The heroic fights in particular, when you figure out the method you will realise it really WAS your strategy that was not working. That being said, some of the more novel fights are a bit too experimental (in particular the hidden boss that you need a red egg to access, while memorable and fun for a first time experience, there is no way anyone will want to fight that boss twice (simply for the chore of it)). The story is moving and the ending A (the only one I've experience so far) felt like an appropriate end (it did feel a little rushed by the last boss fight, but I loved that cliche where those two people turn up). Some of the more punishing concepts in the game are not as bad as you might initially think. You're punished for dying too many times (it has an important story impact) but you can negate the negative effects by challenging bosses to rematches (they have significantly higher levels and expanded or different skill sets).  Spoilers incoming, if you rematch five heroic bosses (the rematches are referred to as heroic fights) you will no longer be punished for dying in game (although you can still earn the negatives from certain magical attacks later in the game). They even give you four easier heroic fights early on (there are five that are really easy, but you have to wait a while to unlock the fifth one and it's more beneficial to go after Bulwark so you can bling up with wings to intimidate other heroic bosses). So, if you are not fond of in game constraints, show a little patience (wait till you're about level 75 if it's your first time playing) then go fight heroic spear girl, owl dude, phoenix chick, sniper lass and Bulwark, boom, no more in game constraints.  Also, there are some things that you may not figure out until it's too late. When you first reach inner sanctuary, kill that green lizard guy, he murders the NPCs you rescue and some of the other vendors and doesn't even give you a reward like he claims to (instead he gets preachy). He doesn't even fight back, so ice him the second you see him (he only sells abilities and there is a vendor not that far into the game that will sell skills that are lost due to NPCs becoming unavailable). When upgrading gear, spend your soul stones like water they are plentiful and easy to obtain and in endless supply. Do not spend your immortal stones, you get a very limited supply and they are very much finite. You can pick a weapon fairly early, damage doesn't really vary within a weapon type, it's more about the special abilities that come attached.

[h2]Well...[/h2]  That was something I totally didn't expected from a game I was just thinking to try for years but always was looking the other way. Honestly i don't know how much game was changed with afterlife update which was released like 2 years ago, but I can say for sure the end result was multiple tiers above of all expectations.  [u][b]The visuals and voice acting[/b][/u]  are 2 things I had expectations for to be good in this game, and damn right it was. A lot of unique locations with different themes and amazing backgrounds to them. [u][b]Voice acting[/b][/u] was really good for a game of that caliber, you can feel character emotions flowing through their speech. But obviously you cant have a game with just that, and here where Death's Gambit starts to surprise.  [u][b]Sound effects[/b][/u] for weapons swinging, blocking and parrying are satisfying to hear.  [u][b]Music[/b][/u] knows when to be inspiring or sound desperate, it has chill\relaxing music for hubs and can be silly when required.   Game provides player with multiple [b]class choices[/b] each with unique [b]talents tree[/b], plus multiple [b]weapon types[/b] with its own movesets. [u][b]Combat[/b][/u] mostly inspired by souls-like games, but [b]Boss fights[/b] have a lot of variety and decent mix of attacks that forces player to adapt. For example, some boss attacks can be dodged easily but others might be able to catch you up with follow up, so that's where [b]perfect block\parry[/b] comes in handy. Yes its 2 different mechanics, while have similarities they have pros and cons. Perfect block just requires you to click the button the moment attack has to hit you, no delays or anything but some bosses have counter to that by requiring you to do that multiple times to actually get a counter attack out of it. Parry can ignore that rule of multiple successful blocks and hits harder while healing you, but downside it isn't instant and puts you in animation lock.  [u][b]Level design[/b][/u] isn't the best in the genre but it fills the requirement of allowing player to [b]explore[/b] multiple paths from the start of the game as long as you able find the upgrades to do so. And now to the aspect I was caught off guard the most, [u][b]characters and story[/b][/u]. Out of all things I didn't expected for a game to provide such likeable characters with so little words while keeping intrigue to the finale.  [h2]I can give this game 8.5\10 score without any regrets[/h2]

A decent metroidvania that had GREAT redemption arc with the FREE Afterlife patch, making it a must play for lovers of the genre! It ticks all the right boxes, story is pretty good with multiple endings, although i'd say that its kinda hard to get the TRUE ending without looking at some guides on a first playthrough which is something I don't usually approve of. Combat is also great depending on how you choose to approach the game, soundtrack is phenomenal and kinda reminds me of Dark Souls 1, not any other but 1. Some boss encounters are kinda mid AF, especially their heroic counterparts - they are so overtuned its just bad design but you aren't forced to do them so this gets a pass from me. The first time I played Death's Gambit was back when it originally came out in 2018 and I remember not liking it very much, but 3 years later the Afterlife version was released, I've heard good things about it and I decided to buy and play it. I don't remember how much I've played of the original version, but it wasn't for long, probably about a few hours. Anyway, the Afterlife version is clearly better cuz I almost finished it (at least NG), but... at the same time, despite the fact that I've played it for ~ 23 hrs... I just don't like (let alone love) it very much for a variety of reasons:  1) Character progression and skill trees are boring af - a lot of the skills in the skill trees are +2/4% to this and that with if's and but's and a few faster animations - you can even buy skills to use with various weapons outright without even spending skill points - you need to meet stat level requirements for some of the weapons though (Strength, Finesse etc.)  2) There's no clear character classes with skill trees focusing on one specific playstyle (sword and shield for example), so you're forced to spend skill points on something else you don't even need or use, just to get something you need - if you're not using shield for example to block attacks, you can't skip some skills to get to the other ones you need. Also, warp orb teleportation is just stupid  3) By the time I got to the final area, I've got 8 unspent skill points and I haven't even picked a second class (not to mention advanced). This goes to show just how useless these skill trees are and not really needed for the story progression at all, you'll be fine with just 5 skills that you've unlocked for your warrior class (for example). It's good to have options, but give that you don't really need most of them to progress through the game, makes these options superficial  4) Combat is mid to average, nothing special really and surface level at best. I mean, sure, you can dash, ground slam, parry attacks and stuff, but it's very unsatisfying visually, sound-wise and in terms of how it feels (except maybe ground slam), that it makes no difference tbh.  5) Horrible backtracking and exploration - you only get fast travel at basically the end of the game and there's ZERO reasons to go back to previous areas by the time you get it. Who thought this was a good idea? There's literally ZERO reasons or explanations as to why it should be like that. Horrible, just horrible design  6) Terrible torture location with spikes, rotating blades and other stupid crap which can't be explained by logic and common sense. Why do some devs keep adding this horrible crap to metroidvanias? WHY? FOR WHAT PURPOSE?!  7) Meh bosses for the most part and heavy reliance on DPS builds. There's a few exceptions of course (like Ione and Endless for example), but one of the bosses are just a regular enemy later on in the game and others are simply not that interesting  8) Level grind and stupid design of locking true ending behind heroic bosses with tons of HP and 2-3 hit kills - you're locked to lvl 150 in NG btw  9) The game just doesn't feel like a clear vision of something great, rather glued together bits and pieces from various games that can't provide a compelling, memorable and interesting enough game as a whole, even after it was redesigned after original launch back in 2018  10) There's a few interesting characters and VA is good, but a lot of the characters felt underdeveloped, hense you don't care about them that much - I won't get into much details to prevent spoilers  11) Bad loot and needless crafting - there's literally only 2 things you can craft (two weapons) and everything else locked behind the end game, so it's stupid and pointless af, especially if you don't like the game very much and won't be playing NG+ but even still... man. As for loot, there's only boring weapons and gear with miniscule stat boosts and weapon upgrades are awful - +5 to DMG at best after each level up and you need to grind for rare resources after lvl 5, which makes it even more boring and awful  12) Horse is pointless and only in the game to make backtracking less of a pain in the ( + ), but it fails completely at doing that, cuz nothing can beat fast travel.  Why have I played the game for ~23 hrs if I don't like it very much, you may ask? Well, I desperately wanted to find and see something in the game which could probably change my mind, but for the life of me... I just could not find it, no matter how hard I tried. I mean, the game looks good visually, but I don't think that it has a very distinct art-design and vision of the world as a whole and that's why it just looks basic and bland most of the time, not to mention lack of any atmosphere and mood tbh. Soundtrack is good, but I can hardly call it the best in the genre. Ah, it's sad really, but it is how it is.  Honestly, go buy and play The Last Faith and Blasphemous games instead, they're far, FAR better games and WAY BETTER metroidvanias, even though the first Blasphemous has its issues as well (all of which were fixed in the second game). It's one thing to strive for ambitions and achieve great things, but the other (and it's a much more important one, MUCH MORE IMPORTANT) to actually have the means, people, talent, time and ability to go beyond your personal needs to actually do this ambition justice and make it right. Death's Gambit imho is the prime example of how it's done wrong.  Sometimes it's way better to just be less ambitious (if you don't have what I've mentioned above), cuz it allows you to make better games with great gameplay mechanics and design, even if you'll lose a bit of originality in the process. It doesn't matter if you borrow ideas from other games, what matters is how you're using them to make them better in your game using your own ideas as well. It's a much safer route for sure, but as long as the end result is great, memorable and interesting, that's what matters the most and unfortunately it's not about Death's Gambit and its dev team.

客观来说，这是一部作者个人风格非常浓郁的游戏，这既是优点也是缺点，很典型的独立游戏。 1. 游戏最突出的优点是音乐和美术，尤其是美术。在像素风游戏里算得上是顶尖水准了。在阴暗的剑与魔法背景下，作者创造性地运用了大量鲜艳的颜色，整个游戏的画风独树一帜，你把宣传片扔到视频集锦里就会有不少人问这是什么游戏。 2. 游戏的剧情和演出令人印象深刻，几场关键的boss战非常震撼，不同结局的安排也可圈可点。游戏的整体世界架构完全值得更丰富的细节。 3. 游戏的战斗系统中规中矩，武器的类型还算丰富，但是数量太少，有些boss设计的很不错，也有一般的，整体来说属于同类游戏的平均水准。 4. 游戏最主要的缺点是，到后期作者很明显是做嗨了，无论是剧情方面还是战斗和解谜方面，都缝合了太多东西，甚至出现了一些比较突兀的Meta元素和门槛过高的ARG解谜。能看出来作者想要把自己喜欢的东西一股脑都加进这个游戏里面，但是玩起来真的有一种感觉，作者不停在炫耀，我草你看我这个东西牛不牛比，实际上如果能够精简一些东西，游戏还能更上一层楼。 5. 游戏的很多细节略欠打磨，比如任务指引方面，NPC剧情方面，物品制作方面，给人的体验比较一般。 总体来说仍然是个好游戏，适合对类银河恶魔城游戏有一定基础，能够对上作者脑电波的硬核玩家。

"Death's Gambit: Afterlife to nie tylko gra, to mistyczna podróż przez mroczną fantastykę w pikselowej oprawie, która może być nieco specyficzna, ale idealnie wpasowuje się w klimat tego wyjątkowego tytułu. Wzorowana na klasykach jak Castlevania i Dark Souls, ta platformówka przesiąknięta jest atmosferą, która przywołuje wspomnienia i wyzwania z minionych lat.  Pixel art, choć może nie być dla każdego, dodaje grze unikalnego uroku, tworząc równocześnie szereg soczyście mięsistych i przykuwających wzrok lokacji. To jakbyś wcielił się w bohatera fantastycznej opowieści, gdzie każdy krok może zakończyć się nie tylko potyczką, ale także odkryciem nowego fragmentu intrygującej historii.  Dodatkowo, rozszerzenie Afterlife stanowi prawdziwą ucztę dla fanów, zwiększając poziom trudności, zawartość i głębokość samej gry. Muzyka, pełna wzruszających i mrocznych dźwięków, doskonale podkreśla klimat, a momenty filozoficzne w historii przyprawiają o dreszcze i chwilami sięgają głęboko do serca gracza. Death's Gambit: Afterlife to nie tylko gra, to doświadczenie, które wciąga na wiele godzin, pozostawiając niezapomniane wrażenia."

神中神，10分可以给9分，整体算是很优秀的类恶魔城。 首先音乐美术方面纯纯满分，没的黑，特别是中央庇护所和来世这两个图，相当有沉浸感。不过这游戏代码有点一坨，主要就是那个坐火的加载有点奇怪的，不管是羽毛回城还是换区都几乎感受不到加载，坐雕像的加载就莫名的长，还有其他例如加点，神秘头盔能在坐雕像的时候把自己烧死，有些boss演出换区的时候或多或少都有点怪，不过还在容忍范围内，总之程序员真应该给音乐美术人员磕一个。 游戏性方面还是相当好玩的，各类build成型后都有各自的爽点，武器装备也很多样。不过平衡性做的不太好，大部分build都是围绕那几个强力天赋构建的，很多武器也没什么用，加上高周目数值在来世版本后仍是灾难，玩到底就是光箭火书。相比较低周末有趣的build和小配合更多，玩起来我认为要更舒服。 然后boss演出和一些角色的塑造可以说是同类作品少有的优秀。所有boss除了前期几个送菜的基本上都有各自的独特机制，同时一定程度上贴合背景，占星术士后的boss演出动画更是全部拉满，也少有那些无法反映的粪招，打起来还是挺爽的。角色的塑造特指死神，我只能说死神是真的懂哲理的，包括死神侍从在内，一路上完全可以说是主角的人生导师，尤其是最后B结局来到来世，结合之前对于生死来世的探讨，如果能玩到B结局的话一定会给一个深刻的映像。而母亲的塑造在来世版本后感觉反而变差了，在老版本主角死亡后会有更多的关于母亲的回忆，来支撑起一个更饱满的形象，新版很多时候感觉就是嗯？而且meta要素感觉有的加多了，观感有点尬。 再说一点不合理的，这游戏对收集党很不友好啊，首先背包有格子限制，大部分物品堆叠限制也严重，加之也没有箱子去存放物品，就导致虽然洗点无限洗，但是想玩花活的话白白会有一堆东西占格子，就有点难受。 还有那个解密纯逆天，直接找攻略吧，割裂感有点严重。 dlc的问题，dlc内容好像就一个竞技场和跳跳乐，竞技场主要就可以刷钱，还有bossrush可以打，再+一个boss艾什，艾什纯纯臭豆腐，机制一坨，只能弹反打，失误一次基本寄，英雄模式再送3个小逆天，弹不好感觉就是史，但弹的好就很爽。 跳跳乐硬跳的话难到爆，但逃课手段还是很多的，首先打完艾什就有无限跳光环，大部分地方就都能强过了，配合跳跃无敌帧和下砸无敌的天赋就更好打，再洗一套血牛玩暗战戟能砍虫子就脚打了。总之dlc完全不建议直接入，内容不多，质量不高，能接受再考虑。 总之这游戏缺点还是很多的，不过优秀的部分足够优秀，而且相当吸引我，所以我给9分，实际上想自己嗯解铸币解密可以扣5分，喜欢全收集的玩家可以扣2分，战斗方面就酌情增减看个人了

It's a decent 2d souls-like, but the game uses bosses as recycled enemies later in the game.  This tends to end up being pretty annoying and repetitive.  It's not a bad game but it's also not a great game.  It hovers around the decent to good range.  There are some different play styles that you can try with your normal strength, finesse (agility), or magic build.  There is some weapon and attack variation through the different gear to be found throughout the game.    However, putting a parasite that goes directly into your inventory was just a horrible design decision.  There is no way in hell that I find searching through my inventory for a parasite any fun.  Mind you this is as your life drains and the game action still rolls in the background as you fiddle through the menu screens.  This design alone was enough to dampen the small amounts of fun that i was having which ultimately makes me feel that this is more in the "decent" range than "good" range.     I only would recommend on a steep sale.  There are much better alternatives to try.  *Edit*   I slept on it for a night and attempted to play again the next day.  I ended up un-installing.  The game progressively gets worse the farther you get.   The enemies are now all recycled bosses that are stacked next to each other.   Not to mention a bunch of cheap hidden traps thrown in.  Hard to recommend this game at all.   There is hardly any fun to be had.  It ends up being a repetitive snoozefest.

I loved 3/4 of the game for how grim the overall tone was, the characters being interesting and sometimes questionable, the combat, aesthetic and specially the atmosphere (specially when you get to an specific realm) being well done. Sadly, there's a huge grind towards the end in order to get the true ending. You fight overtuned and bloated versions of bosses you already fought, and makes it tedious for some builds devolving into DPS checks. It's inelegant and completely breaks the pacing.   I enjoyed my time with the game, and I wish the endgame for the true ending wasn't a grind of reused bosses with bigger health, bigger damage output and a lot of dangerous hazards in the vicinity.

Started off pretty fun, but the gameplay mechanics became tedious over time. The boss fights went from overly easy at the start to being incredibly overtuned near the end. I eventually got stuck because I HAD to respec to progress, but in order to do so, I'd have to farm up gear when swapping from finesse to strength, or I'd be permanently losing about 30% of my weapon damage. The overall ambiance, questline, and voice acting was solid, so I'm pretty disappointed that they went overboard like this. Even if I can win, it's just not fun trying to cheese bosses that have overtuned mechanics.  Edit after completing the game:  So, I was bothered enough by not being able to finish the story that I looked up how to edit the save file and gave myself 500 stats in each attribute so I could complete the game. Honestly, the story was worth it. The atmosphere, the emotional journey, the various endings, and the characters were all high quality. It's truly unfortunate that so many people probably never made it to the end, because it was remarkably well-done.   That said, I still don't recommend playing this game since I had to edit my save file to even reach the end. I'm not new to hard games, I've played every souls game, completed Lies of P, and even gave the new 2023 Lords of the Fallen a recommendation when so many disliked it. While I could have beaten the boss I was stuck on with some grinding and loot collection, what about the bosses after that? No way, I would have gotten stuck somewhere, especially since you have to beat the heroic bosses to reach the "good" ending. Holy smokes, even with 500 of each stat, some of those bosses were still hitting me like a truck.  It's not a game I regret buying and playing, but it's not a game I'd recommend to anyone. If you're a highly skilled gamer who loves pain and good stories, check it out. For everyone else, either be ready to edit your save file or give this game a pass.  I hope the developer does more games in the future because of how amazing so many aspects of this game were. Just....next time, remember that not all of us are Dark Souls challenge runners.

While Deaths Gambit Afterlife might not break new ground in the saturated realm of 2D Metroidvania Soulslikes, it's undeniably a great game. The visuals are great, and the combat is satisfying. Despite the relatively small game world, it manages to have many diverse areas. Perhaps stretching out the world a bit more would have been better over compressing everything into a confined space. The bosses are decent, though not exceptionally interesting.  For those seeking an extra challenge, there's an option to face a massivly harder version of each boss. Unfortunately, these encounters often felt poorly designed, relying on increased numbers and introducing gimmicks like restricted healing, reduced visibility, overlapping attacks, or excessive minion spawns.  In summary, I recommend the game as a solid Metroidvania experience, but if you are a completionist aiming to tackle all optional challenges you should think twice about starting this game.

Death's Gambit: Afterlife is yet another of literally thousands of 2D retro pixel platformers infesting Steam and lowering the average quality of all video games everywhere.  In fairness, this Playstation 4 game (which for some reason has been put on Steam) is arguably one of the better 2D retro pixel platformers... this is a lot like being fairly tall for a midget.  No prizes there.  A lot of effort went into this 2D retro pixel platformer... which is a lot of time wasted polishing the proverbial.  It's still gonna be what it is, no matter how shiny it might look.   There's a slew of defects and gamer hostile design decisions here.  From a technical perspective, the game doesn't meet basic minimum requirements that most PC gamers expect as standard.  A choice was made to use obsolete, decades old retro pixel "art" as a substitute for contemporary PC graphics.  Worse, not all elements of the graphics are pixelcrap, which shows the developer could sometimes do their job properly... so there's jarring, mismatched visual elements, and the pixel "art" is rendered completely inauthentic from a "retro" perspective.  The developer made the game look bad, on purpose.  What a joke.   While there are options to change the resolution for the game, all this does is scale up the simplistic 2D art assets used to make the game, which makes little or no difference to the graphics quality.  Without any other substantial graphics tweaks, it's not possible for gamers to improve the lacklustre 2D visuals.  The developers didn't design the game for modern gaming PCs, as such the display resolution caps out at around 1440p, there's no support for the state-of-the-art 4K or higher resolutions.  The game simply won't look right on modern gaming displays due to this failure on the part of the developers.  Because this is designed for consoles, it carries a number of deliberate design deficiencies.  Compromises were made to cater to the inferior console gaming appliances that the game was designed for.  These are unfortunate compromises and limitations that PC gamers shouldn't be forced to accept, but it's evident that PC was a second thought for the console-centric developers.  The game is deficient as a result of these choices, and would have been so much better without the handicaps that designing games for consoles forces upon a game.  Once more, console peasants have made gaming worse for everyone.  In a sheer insult to PC gamers, the developer has locked out keyboard and mouse controls on this game if you have any gaming controller (HOTAS, racing wheel, pedals etc).  If you pull out all of your controllers it will unlock keyboard input, where you can then instruct the game that you are not a console peasant and do not want to use a console peasant children's toy controller.  The developer deliberately made this input choice as obscure as possible to try punish PC gamers for not being console peasants.  This is completely and utterly unacceptable.   These technical defects push this game below acceptable standards for any modern PC game.  Death's Gambit: Afterlife has the gamer gouging price of $20 USD, for which you could easily get any number of older AAA games.  They're seriously charging $20 for a console game developed by people who don't even know how to do 3D graphics because it's too hard for them.  This is also competing with over 9,000 free games available on Steam, many of them far better than this paid product.  Given the defects and quality issues with the game, coupled with the unrealistic price, this is impossible to recommend.

If your looking for another great 2d soulslike metroidvania with many classes and built in modifiers like random items, then deaths gambit is definitely a must have.

I originally played the original game thanks to Gamepass, once per class (3 times total) so keep that in mind for my feedback. It took me around 40 hours for my first replay of this game, specifically using the Acolyte of Death class focusing on finesse and vitality using Scythe's and spears/Halberds.  While it's not a perfect experience, in its current form it is my favourite metroidvania period.  Pros - A very unique and interesting art style, not as dark as some soulslikes but with a captivating story that honestly surprised even me. - Large variety of fun weapons and playstyles, especially with the extra classes added to this version (I really like that you can get a feel for the base classes before committing on your first playthrough).  - Good QOL added and the extra content overall is quite good even if it took a little bit of extra adjusting from the vanilla game.  - Reasonable length, especially for the base price so even factoring in one playthrough you're still getting good value for money and then you have new game plus for the long term value enjoyers!   Cons - The Heroic boss battles. While some were quite enjoyable and epic (Forgotten Gaian, Endless and Death Knight in particular), the late game fights felt more tedious and frustrating than I would like, with either too many enemies on screen/mechanics to keep track of causing you to die in frustration or simply forcing you to cheese the boss and move on (Eldritch Council, The Crow especially).   - The map design feels a little worse than vanilla, there were several times I had to consult a guide because of simply missing something very obscure such as a hidden wall, on top of lack of fast travel which often felt annoying (yes I know there are plenty of good shortcuts but it doesn't make sense to leave that option to literally before the final area).   - The hitboxes on some attacks, both with player weapons and enemies can be a bit inconsistent, getting clipped from fullscreen thinking an attack has missed is never a good feeling or how difficult it was to hit enemies with an aerial attack using a horizontal weapon such as the spear.  - It may have just been unfortunate timing but there was times my inputs felt like they were being eaten, especially fighting some bosses and attempting a block or parry leading to a few cheap deaths despite my best efforts.  Tl;dr   It's a good game that has great potential to be made even better, highly recommended especially if you are able to get it on sale as I did.

Ein Deal mit dem Tod und wir sterben immer wieder  Hier geht’s zu meinem Waetsch's Play: https://youtu.be/NyFlrF3oLV4  In diesem Pixelgrafik Soulslike kämpfen wir als wiedererweckter Kämpfer für den Tod selbst. Die Grafik ist dabei ganz pixelig hübsch und auch der Sound gefällt. Die englische Sprachausgabe ist gut vertont und stimmungsvoll. Die Texte sind in gut verständlichem Deutsch, was leider auch keine Selbstverständlichkeit ist. Gesteuert wird hier wahlweise per Gamepad oder Maus/ Tastatur. Ein fließender Wechsel ist scheinbar nicht möglich und muss im Menü umgestellt werden (es gibt schlimmeres). Die Gamepad-Steuerung funktioniert ganz gut, auch wenn mir die Kämpfe, besonders die Bosskämpfe etwas zufällig vorkommen. Witzig ist die Charakterauswahl und auch sonst wird die Geschichte gut transportiert. Da man bei einem Tod "nur" eine Feder (quasi ein Heiltrank) verliert und diesen wieder aufsammeln kann (auch mehrfach, wenn man vor dem Einsammeln das Zeitliche gesegnet hat), ist der Tod hier nicht ganz so schmerzhaft. Eine nette 2D-Sidescrolling-Soulslike-Erfahrung, die ganz spaßig zu spielen ist.   Folgt mir auch gerne bei Steam: https://store.steampowered.com/curator/41912197-waetschs-play

There are no big problems with this game but a TON of smaller issues that accumulate in a negative experience. To list a few: Room transitions push you back into the other room causing multiple loading screens, dmg values from enemies/bosses are inconsistent (most attacks deal 40 to 50 but some deal 300+ while in the same encounter), groundpound-able floors sometimes invisible parrying/blocking sometimes works from neither side and sometimes from both sides...  And as a little bonus, the devs dont like the player being able to respec. It got added after a lot of negative feedback but you can only respec twice (without the game telling you so)

So much gimmick bosses that limit your tactics to the same repeated ♥♥♥♥ because anything else is easily prevented. Everyone can interrupt your skills, but you can't interrupt theirs... Bosses and even some enemies have attacks that cover the entire ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ screen, bosses spawn infinite mobs during fights, and said mobs are pure damage sponges, to the point that when one finally dies, the boss has already spawnned another one. Bosses use special skills while moving, you can't even use or alternate between consumables unless you're standing completely still on the ground. Every enemy and boss' special attacks charge and release much faster than yours, and if you were releasing yours and got attacked during it, not only does the skill has no effect, but it also counts as used, so you can't try again until charging it. Just ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ lovely. I could go on and on, but the gist is that while the game is a fun metroidvania, it has some tremendously unfair design, a clear reminder that while you can copy soulslike mechanics, if you don't know how to implement them properly, the result is a mess that doesn't leave any satisfaction after being overcome. If I have plenty of interesting skills but I'm always punished for trying to use them since enemies just span ♥♥♥♥ constantly, obviously I'll simply ignore the skills and span my basic attacks as well. Barely any enemies have a painstate anyways, and it usually requires you to use special skills to cause said state and interrup their actions, it's just not worth it. But by far, the most agregious ♥♥♥♥ I saw was a wolf like enemy that hits you with red beams of energy, even if you're behind him. It's ridiculous to see the attack clip trhough his own body to hit you behind him, even though his hands are pointing foward as a ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ mime. I don't recomend this, and being indie doesn't redeem the awful design choices made here, but I'll beat this awful game anyway so as to prove this opinion isn't due to a lack of skill on my part, but a lack of quality on the game itself.

TL;DR: è un capolavoro. Posso dire con certezza che i recensori online NON L'HANNO GIOCATO. Dare 6 a questo gioco è un crimine contro l'umanità.  Indietro nel tempo: guardiamo un video di VaatyVidya, io e la mia ragazza ci innamoriamo della colonna sonora di Alex Roe, esploriamo altre sue canzoni e arriviamo al suo album di Death's Gambit. Compro immediatamente il gioco e lo divoro in 3 giorni.  Questa è stata l'esperienza videoludica più bella in anni (probabilmente più di Elden Ring, senza esagerare). Il gioco è di una bellezza indescrivibile, sia dal lato artistico che filosofico. Ci sono sicuramente più pro che contro, ma premetto che ho giocato solo alla versione Afterlife che a quanto ho capito ha rivoluzionato il gioco e lo ha migliorato sotto tanti aspetti. Credo sia uno dei motivi per cui le review siano così tanto difformi tra di loro.  Sto facendo questa recensione dopo aver finito la [spoiler]Ending B, dovendo ancora fare la A[/spoiler].   AGGIORNAMENTO: ho finito la Ending [spoiler]A, che tra l'altro è la vera ending del gioco, sono un cretino[/spoiler], e tutto quello scritto qui sotto vale x2. Giocone. Giocone assoluto.  PRO: Musica. È la colonna sonora più bella che abbia mai sentito, non sto esagerando. L'alternarsi di ost tristi con ost epiche creano sempre l'accompagnamento più adatto per il personaggio che si sta affrontando o per la zona che si sta attraversando. Storia. Criptica al punto giusto, affascinante e avvincente. Tocca benissimo il tema della morte in modi diretti (con dialoghi) o indiretti (eventi di gioco, lore). Bellissima. Grafica. La pixel art non può piacere a tutti, ma se vi piace non potete non apprezzare il gioco. Ambientazioni. Ce ne sono di ogni tipo, davvero. Dalla più bassa e creepy alla più alta  Build. Due classi selezionabili su 7 con i loro rami di talenti + un ramo base + un ramo avanzato, in più fin dall'inizio c'è la possibilità di resettare stats e talenti. C'è da sbizzarrirsi. Nemici. Non esiste un singolo nemico unfair. Tutti, boss compresi, possono essere più o meno difficili ma mai sleali o buggati. Ogni boss (a parte i primi 2 circa) ha dei movimenti caratteristici e/o delle meccaniche uniche che rendono ogni fight stimolante, non vengono mai ripetute le stesse meccaniche.  CONTRO: Interfaccia. È parecchio macchinosa, l'inventario si riempie presto di roba utile e inutile, impossibile da ordinare manualmente e tedioso da navigare. La mappa nella zona del [spoiler]Grey Wanderer[/spoiler] è praticamente inutile e mi ha fatto venir voglia di tirare giù i muri. Comandi. Purtroppo non tutte le keybindings sono modificabili, quindi utilizzare lo schema default per la tastiera (a parte qualche lieve modifica) è quasi obbligatorio. A volte (non spesso) sembra anche che il gioco non registri bene gli input, non so bene perché. Bast. Chi sa sa. [spoiler]Ho maledetto tutti i santi, ma solo alla fine ho capito quanto centrasse con i temi trattati dal gioco, e come fosse un catalizzatore perfetto per spingere il nostro pg a scegliere l'Ending B. Non è un vero contro, ma perdere tutti gli npc de botto può rovinare l'esperienza per alcuni[/spoiler]. Performance. Sarebbe piacevole andare oltre il cap dei 60 fps, ma quella pare una limitazione dell'engine per cui c'è poco da fare. Bilanciamento. Alcune classi (così come alcune armi) sono oggettivamente migliori di altre, e non potendo respeccare la prima classe non si può godere di perk incredibili come quello dell'AoD o del Soldier.  Oh, giocatelo. Non gli avrei dato una lira, ora è tra i miei giochi preferiti.  [spoiler]theeldritchhand.com[/spoiler]

Acho que antes de considerar minha análise como "legal, vou comprar" dentre tantas outras, tem uns avisos que eu gostaria de fazer. Sim, o jogo é bom e divertido, tem uma sonoridade ambiente ótima, uma pixel art bem bacana, uma historinha de fundo e tudo mais. É um metroidvania souls-like 2d bem sóbrio, com bastante conteúdo. Só que, como muitas pessoas que acompanharam a campanha de arrecadação já alertaram que o jogo não trouxe muito bem aquilo que prometeu, eu olharia esse game com cautela.   Se for pegar, pegue em uma promoção. Tranquilamente é um jogo que irá te divertir, mas apesar de eu ter recomendado eu acho que ele não é para mim. Gosto de metroidvania, mas admito que fiquei meio de saco cheio já na metade do jogo e acabei desinstalando. Quem sabe em uma futura oportunidade eu termine ele, mas de momento talvez nunca mais pegue para jogar novamente. Preciso terminar a série Metroid e jogar infelizmente leva tempo, então estou direcionando minha atenção a outros jogos.

Absolutely beautiful game, both in visuals an narrative. I really felt connected to the characters, their plights, regrets, and joy. The combat can be enjoyable and it is overall fun to master. Many of the bosses are really well done, smooth and reasonably difficult.   However, there are many glaring issues with the game mechanics. Some bosses seem incredibly easy, while some unreasonably hard. Even at level 150 with many hours experience. This isn't me complaining of a skill issue, there is a genuine difference between "fun hard" and "annoying hard".   The lack of weapons makes it feel like you have no real options on what to use. As the Acolyte of Death class, there is practically only one scythe you are going to be using the entire game.  There is some additional annoyance in the vagueness of much of the lore. This could just be me missing some things, but there seem to be plenty of unexplained events, people, creatures, etc. The main plot is explained well, however I came out with many questions. I do hope they make a second game laying out more of the lore.  In spite of all the problems, however, Deaths Gambit is definitely one of, if not my favorite souls-like games, and one of my favorite games overall.
